issues.description:
a End user informed QST that sa213 performance is lower than the others.
What must we confirm server logs for investigating the cause?

at first, NEC confirmed power capping status same as HPL performance troubleshooting.
However, there is no power capping limit.

[necse02@msv8 2.1.78]$ java -jar ./GbtUtility-2.1.78.jar -H sa213-bmc -U admin -P *** limit get
*******************************************
*     GIGABYTE(TM) GbtUtility v2.1.78     *
*******************************************
Get BMC platform information :
{
  "Generation" : "AST26xx",
  "Chip ID" : "AST2600",
  "Architecture" : "x86",
  "Platform Vendor" : "Intel",
  "Platform Name" : "Birch Stream"
}
[10.20.132.13] Completion code     : 0x80
[sa213-bmc] No Active Set Power Limit.
limit process complete ... (1/1)
*******************************************************************
*       GIGABYTE(TM) CLI "limit" Command Executions Summary       *
*******************************************************************
Target IP:      | Command Name: | Status: | Total elapsed times:
sa213-bmc       | limit         | Success | 0.292 sec.
Total success nodes : 1  fail nodes : 0
*******************************************************************


Firmware:
BIOS Version: F15
BMC Version: 82.01.03
CMC Version:

notes.text:
What criteria did the end user use to determine that this server has low performance?
Have any relevant data or screenshots been provided?

Could you first confirm with the end user and provide the relevant information before treating this as a issue and submitting an EIP ticket?

The program used for performance comparison is HPL.
The performance values of other nodes are around 1.5e+04, but sa213's value is around 5.7e+03.

The settings we have checked are as follows:

  1) No power capping is configured
　([sa213-bmc] No Active Set Power Limit.)
  2) No CPU socket or core is offline
　256 cores are active, the same as other nodes.
  3) The CPU clock is not different from other nodes
　- The governor is set to "performance"
    - # grep MHz /proc/cpuinfo | sort | uniq -c
    255 cpu MHz         : 2000.000
      1 cpu MHz         : 3798.056
  4) Memory capacity is normal and there is no DIMM is offline
  5) No errors found in SEL or OS syslog (messages)
　- These logs have been uploaded
  6) The OS has been rebooted, but performance remains low

Information to be collected next:

  7) lspci -tv / lspci -vvv
  8) BMC syslog
　./gbtipmitool-linux -H x.x.x.x -U admin -P <password> bmcutil export system log

If there are any other logs that should be collected, please let me know.

No abnormalities were found in the lspci results. I have attached the results. 
I have also collected the results from the BMC syslog. Since I have attached the results, please analyze them.

After checking all logs, we did not find any events related to power capping, overheating, or anything that could affect performance.

Could you first try to re-seat this node (by AC power) and then observe if the performance issue still occurs?

Additionally, could you also provide the following information:

1.The commands used for HPL testing

2.The reproduction rate (whether it occurs 100% of the time or randomly)

I have collected the following logs. Please check the results. 
　ipmitool -H <host_name> -U admin -P password -t 0x26 -b 0 sel list

Next, I will replace the node to check the impact on performance degradation.

Based on the current logs, no clear anomalies can be identified.

If possible, please help monitor the power consumption of each node (sdr ND*_PWR) to check whether there is any significant drop during HPL runs.
Later, if swap tests confirm that the issue is related to a specific node and an RMA is required, could you also assist in providing HPL commands or relevant testing methods that can demonstrate performance degradation on a single node? This will help with further analysis.

Has testing been scheduled after replacing this node?
Or is there an expected date for the replacement?

As this ticket has had no updates for over one month, we will proceed with closing it for now.

If there are any further questions or if additional assistance is needed, please feel free to open a new ticket at any time. Thank you.

NEC wants GCT to clearly provide the troubleshooting procedure before closing this issue.
Also, NEC would like to know which log files and data should be collected when low performance occurs.
Just want to quickly confirm — after the reboot, the issue hasn't been seen again on the customer side, right? Or is it still reproducible so far?

Right!
After rebooting, we have not heard that the same server experienced decreased performance.
However, the customer has not verified the performance, so they may not have found any low performance.

issues.summary: Megware XV23-ZX0-AAJ1 BMC MAC address AC後跳成另一組
issues.description:
Hi Knut,

Megware那邊一台XV23-ZX0剛剛回報說，BMC MAC address在ac cycle後會跳成另外一組，客戶提供的LOG如下，當前BMC是23.08.32。這台能用以往的sample.sh方式燒MAC address進去嗎?

Dear all, we need immediate support please, as we are struggling with a XV23-ZX0-AAJ1 (GPG2P5212A0022, OpenBMC-based) for several weeks now. Before, we had several stability issues maybe caused by faulty DIMM, CPU or GPU we just 
sorted out. Now, we've an amber System Status LED, maybe caused by Physical_Scrty sensor Critical. 
 
Unfortunately, I find no way to reset the value (first issue). Furthermore for debugging this issue I upgraded BMC fw to 23.08.32 on the active image via BMC web UI. After update and resulted BMC reset the BMC was in good shape regarding network configuration. 
 
Unfortunately, after an AC cycle the original BMC MAC (10:ff:e0:b6:32:ac) is gone and now seems to be randomly allocated (a2:cf:d9:82:a5:71). How to fix this second issue?



Thank you
B.R.Cliff

The chassis-open SEL log will remain asserted once the cover has been opened.
Even after closing the chassis, it will not automatically deassert.
You must use an IPMI OEM command to clear the SEL after the cover is closed.

The team has try to reproduce the issue but not successful.

Now we don't have a method to recover the system  from current status, so we will arrange the BMC card to you for swap.

Question from the team, any actions be taken during the AC cycle(between the time of pullout/insert the power)?

I don't understand exactly what you mean, but after flashing, there was a time frame of about one minute or so and then I just disconnected the power cord to get the system in a fresh state. At this point the ID button was already dead but I don't know if it worked before. I was not sure that the MAC address change will take place without AC cycle and at the test place I had no access to the DCHP server log. After all PSU LEDs went off, I just reconnected all 4 power cables one by one.

We've inserted the BMC PCB from the second server. There is also no power-on. We see, that the LED_PLD1 both on MB and BMC PCB stays off in comparison to the second server where the LEDs are solid green after maybe 5 seconds or so after AC connect.
I think we should RMA the MB or any other idea?

Please provide the RMA form and we will arrange the spares.

I'll arrange MB and the SCM boards.

Hi Cliff, 
thank you. Enclosed the filled RMA form.
BR, Sabine
詢問RMA號

Hi Cliff,
news about the advanced replacements of MB and the SCM boards? 
Thanks & BR, Sabine

NLS-25C0044

Hi Sabine,

Did you receive the boards?

Thanks

Hi Cliff,
no, we have not received the boards yet. 
BR, Sabine

Hi Sabine,

Got some update.

We're waiting for 1 of the small card from HQ, CSC401.

And the rest CDCX101 and the MB we have stock in RMA.

So in total you will receive 3 boards.

Thanks
問blue小卡的ETA and tracking number

Hi Cliff, in the meantime we have received:
1 x 5CDCX101NR-00-11C  and 
1 x 5MZX3MG0IR-000-30A.

The MBD has been replaced but as you know we have further issues with the system. 
For your analyse we shipped out the failed MBD today via UPS, tracking 1Z6479766852669513. 

PS. we have not replaced the 5CDCX101NR-00-11C yet. 
BR, Sabine

Hi 

Some updates:

1.Unfortunately, after an AC cycle the original BMC MAC (10:ff:e0:b6:32:ac) is gone and now seems to be randomly allocated (a2:cf:d9:82:a5:71). How to fix this?
→How did you check if the BMC MAC address has randomly changed? By using inband ipmitool lan print command?

2.I don't understand exactly what you mean, but after flashing, there was a time frame of about one minute or so and then I just disconnected the power cord to get the system in a fresh state. At this point the ID button was already dead but I don't know if it worked before. I was not sure that the MAC address change will take place without AC cycle and at the test place I had no access to the DCHP server log. After all PSU LEDs went off, I just reconnected all 4 power cables one by one.

→While performed a BMC firmware update and powered off the system after approximately one minute. Could you please clarify which update method was used — via the WebUI or ?

If the update was performed through the WebUI, seeing a message indicating that the upload has completed does not mean that the firmware update itself has finished successfully.

According to the native OpenBMC design, once the firmware image is successfully uploaded to the BMC, it is first stored in the BMC RAM. The BMC then performs a reset, and before the reboot completes, it proceeds to write the firmware image to the flash storage.

In this case, where the BMC image size is approximately 128 MB, it is not possible for the firmware update to complete within one minute. If the customer disconnects power at this stage, the firmware update process is interrupted. As a result, during the next AC power-on, the BMC flash may be left in a corrupted state, and the BMC may fail to execute its code and therefore fail to boot.

In some cases, after waiting for a sufficient period of time, the BMC may boot from the secondary flash (flash2) and enter recovery mode. During this process, the BMC ID and status LEDs will blink. After approximately 15–20 minutes, once the blinking stops, the recovery process is complete, and the system can then switch back to the active BMC image and proceed with a normal boot.

However, based on our observation, it apprears it did not wait long enough before disconnecting the power.

3.We inserted the BMC PCB from the second server, but there is still no power-on. We observed that LED_PLD1 on both the motherboard and the BMC PCB remains off, unlike the second server where the LEDs turn solid green about 5 seconds after AC connection. 
→ we're checking details from CPLD team. Thus the BMC board from 2nd server works well on 2nd server?
     both BMC board from 1st and 2nd server did not work with 1st server?

4.After MB's changed, did system works well and is there any MAC address flapping problem?
5.  we have further issues with the system. → what's the future problems?

Hi Cliff,
system rebooted unexpectedly several times and BMC was not reachable anymore, but we guess it depends on a failed CPU...we keep you updated.
BR, Sabine

1.) Yes, the BMC was accessed in-band via ipmitool lan print 1
2.) No, flashing means the modified script from XV23-ZX0-AAJ1-000.20251128.zi_ sample.nsh:

##### 8>< ######
Bmcprog022.efi DSBSHM MainMAC=10ffe0b632ac ShareMAC=10ffe0b632ad

gigaflash.efi SKU.BIN -sku -2500
##### ><8 #####

There is a wait for BMC boot included, which I waited for. Afterwards I checked/noticed no working ID LED, but the BMC had already requested the correct IP for 10ffe0b632ac which I saw in the DHCP server log later. Unfortunately, after 1-2 minutes I AC-cycled the system to also remove the USB key, but afterwards the BMC. As we discovered later, the SCM board was not broken but the whole mainboard prevent BMC power- or boot-up.

The BMC update was always done via WebUI and no AC-cycled involved until the BMC and/or the OS was reachable again.
As I mentioned the BMC/SCM board is not broken and the update itself was successful.

3. Yes, the first server remained dead both with 1st and 2nd BMC board but the 1st and 2nd BMC board worked in the second server.

4./5. Yes, after MB changed the original/1st BMC board works without issues again and the system powers up. We'll flash all the latest firmware (CPLD, BIOS, BMC) today, after we sorted out a defective CPU yesterday.

Please analyze the defective MB to get an idea if it was a surprise dead or something other related to BMC MAC address change.

Hi Rene, 

Will do.

Thanks
This ticket will focus on tracking analyze result of defected MB.

Customer Megware (EU) Issue ID 7179

* Problem: Megware reports BMC MAC address on XV23-ZX0-AAJ1 (SN: GPG2P5212A0022) changes after AC cycle. BMC version is 23.08.32. Initial MAC was 10:ff:e0:b6:32:ac, now randomly allocated. System also experienced stability issues (DIMM, CPU, GPU) and amber System Status LED (Physical_Scrty Critical). RMA initiated for motherboard and SCM boards due to suspected motherboard failure preventing BMC power-up. Defective motherboard shipped for analysis. BMC update performed via WebUI, with a power cycle initiated prematurely (within 1-2 minutes) after the update appeared to complete. This may have corrupted the BMC flash.
* Next Action: Analyze the defective motherboard to determine the cause of failure and if it’s related to the BMC MAC address issue. Investigate the BMC flash corruption possibility due to premature power cycle.
* LastActivityFromCreation: 90 days
* Status: acknowledged

issues.summary: 客户升级BMC、BIOS和GPU操作遇到的问题
issues.description:
1，1.05.00的bmc同版本升级（保留配置）后也会出现bmc异常，管理口被异常激活
2，BIOS升级（目标版本：1.03.00），重启后
1）部分生效时间长达20min+，待厂商确认
2）部分未生效7台，内部验证中
3，GPU升级
1）升级前HMC异常7台，有故障现象预留设备
2）升级后HMC异常*2台，有故障现象预留设备

Firmware:
BIOS Version: 1.03
BMC Version: 1.05
CMC Version:

notes.text:
1. BMC問題，我們會跟RD確認後回覆
2. BIOS升級問題，更新後第一次重啟會比較久
請提供機器卡在哪一部的畫面，看看是卡在memory initialize還是chipset initialize
也請提供該機器的BMC SEL給RD分析問題
並確認，第一次重啟之後的重啟是不是還是很久
3. GPU升級問題
升級前HMC異常 : 請跟客戶確認是輸入了什麼指令以及什麼樣的異常
升級後HMC異常 : 我們會與NV確認

我們目前有拿到新版的gpufw v1.4.0
因為客戶有指定使用的版本，請先不要使用或部屬下去，請先與客戶確認
gpufw已經放在onedrive上，在G894-SD1的GPU資料夾內

內部初步判定並非BIOS版本問題，與手法可能較有關聯性
建議針對有卡過久的機台選出幾台用BMC web更新方法去比對是否有相同問題，不用utool的方式
另外版本未生效的問題，建議請客戶先確保開機到UEFI/OS後再進行讀取
否則版本資訊會在未同步情況被讀取

custom_fields.value:
Customer Dimension AI (EU) Issue ID 7872

* Problem:
    * BMC: Upgrading to BMC version 1.05 (even with config retention) can activate the management port unexpectedly.
    * BIOS: Upgrade to version 1.03. Some systems take >20 minutes to reboot; 7 systems didn't apply the update. Investigation ongoing.
    * GPU: 7 systems had HMC issues *before* GPU upgrade (faulty units). 2 systems have HMC issues *after* GPU upgrade. New GPU firmware v1.4.0 is available but client has specified a version - do not deploy yet.

* Next Action:
    * BMC: RD to investigate BMC activation issue.
    * BIOS: Provide screenshots of boot process for slow reboots & BMC SEL logs to RD. Confirm reboot times after initial reboot.
    * GPU: Confirm client's input/errors prior to upgrade. NV to investigate post-upgrade HMC issues. Confirm desired GPU firmware version with client.

* LastActivityFromCreation: 0 days
* Status: acknowledged

issues.summary: The BIOS parameters for these motherboards are resetting to default values (NUMA, L3, etc.).
issues.description:
Issue:
The BIOS parameters for these motherboards are resetting to default values (NUMA, L3, etc.).

Temporary Solution:
We have found that shutting down the worker node and unplugging the coin battery cable for 5–10 minutes clears the cache. Upon powering the node back up, P_VBAT returns to > 3V.

Seeking Permanent Solution:
I am looking for clarification on the following:

- Why does this "reset" process bring P_VBAT back to a normal voltage level?
- Is this a long-term fix, or will the low-voltage issue recur? For context, compute-0-12 was reset this way last December, but the issue returned in February 2026.
- Will replacing the battery with a new unit permanently resolve the problem?

Thanks
Terry

Firmware:
BIOS Version: Version: F01
BMC Version: Firmware Revision         : 13.06
CMC Version: N/A

notes.text:
Hello Joshua, 

Happy Friday!  I just wanted to follow up if you got a chance to look at the case yet? Please provide an update at your earliest convenience.

Thanks
Terry

Hi  Joshua, 

Here is the latest update form our customer, 
The sensor log file for all systems after I "reset" the coin battery by unplugging the cables for 10 minutes. 
At this moment, there are 3 worker nodes with "nc/nr" status for low-voltage:
|compute-0-26 | P_VBAT | 2.578 | Volts | nc | na | 2.549 | 2.725 | na | na | na |
|compute-0-30 | P_VBAT | 2.432 | Volts | cr | na | 2.549 | 2.725 | na | na | na |
|compute-0-41 | P_VBAT | 2.696 | Volts | nc | na | 2.549 | 2.725 | na | na | na |


I am also uploading the log: cdp63.allsystems.ipmitool.sensor.list.02242026

Thanks,
Jeff

Hi Joshua,

Can we get an update on whether this is a simple battery failure issue or something more significant?


Thanks,
Jeff

Hi Jeff, this should be only battery failure, not anything more significant. One trial way is to use another system's known good battery and swap it around, the issue will follow the battery. I believe replacing these batteries would be the best case. It might work after some disconnect and re-plugging it back in.

Thanks Andy. who shoudl we contact to get a few of these cabled batteries?

Jeff

You can ask RMA team to provide you with spares, the PN for this system serial number is 11BL5-032032-N0R.

Alternatively, you can search for CR2032 laptop battery or similar on amazon if we don't have it in stock, they should all work except the ones that mention 'inverse' or 'reverse'.

Ok , just to confirm part number 11BL5-032032-N0R is for the cabled Cr2032 battery?


Thanks,
Jeff

Yes, the system with MB SN OK9N9000040 was identified to be using CR2032 battery PN  11BL5-032032-N0R, technically this battery should work for all other systems as well but may need different length depending on the proper placement it goes on.

Andy,

Why would systems lose their bios setting when the voltageon the battry drops below 2.7 while they are setill plugged in?

 customer comment:
"The systems will lose their BIOS setting when the battery voltage drops below 2.7V even though they are plugged into a power source. Thats the problem we have to deal with right now. 
That happened on 6 systems. The customer jobs were running and failed last week because the BIOS parameters were reset to default."

Thanks,
jeff

Hi Jeff,

Is it possible to ask the customer to use a multi-meter on the battery positive (red) and negative (black) pinouts and check it's health? As I know, even older CR2032 batteries should still function and retain BIOS settings even if their voltages drops, however a very small percentage of batteries that do occasional fail may just not be able to retain any charge when the system is power cycled.

Andy,

Andy, 

The customer has flown back to california, but questions remain why would some of the systems lose their bios setting and reset to default settings if the system was never power cycled?

Have you seen where the battery drops below the threshold and the system resets the BIOS settings while still running?

again this is not the case on all systems with battery issue just a few of them. 

Thanks,
jeff

Hello Andy and Team,  

The attached photos show the battery, which appears to be glued to the top of U201.We are just trying to figure out the best way to remove the old batteries. Any suggestions?

Thanks
Terry

custom_fields.value:
Customer Penguin Computing Solutions (USA) Issue ID 7858

* Problem: Multiple worker nodes (compute-0-26, compute-0-30, compute-0-41, and previously compute-0-12) are experiencing BIOS parameter resets to default values. This occurs when the P_VBAT voltage drops below 2.7V, even while plugged into a power source. A temporary fix involves unplugging the coin battery cable for 5-10 minutes, restoring voltage to >3V. The customer reports job failures due to these resets. Initial assessment points to battery failure as the root cause.
* Next Action: Replace the cabled CR2032 battery (PN 11BL5-032032-N0R) on affected systems. Request customer to perform voltage health check with a multimeter. Determine if issue recurs after battery replacement.
* LastActivityFromCreation: 4 days
* Status: assigned

(They have chassis with 3PSUs that include the login nodes and standard one with only 2)

issues.description:
Hi Carlo, Brent,

Thanks for the meeting yesterday, it's always good to have a meeting in-person :)

@Carlo you mentioned a new major version of the GSM server. Can you check this for us? The current online version is really outdated. We also would like to manage fan-profiles centrally, so maybe a feature request for development?
@Carlo @Brent, You said you would look into the possibility to put the MAC-address sticker on the outside of the chassis, currently this sticker is on the inside. We've automated the deployment of servers, and for this we need the MAC-address.
@Brent, @Daniel, regarding the issue with the fan cabling, we're going to do extra checks on other older generation servers. Now that we've put the new servers in production, it's possible to take the server out to do inspections, so let me know if you would like to do this. We can arrange this if needed.

As we discussed yesterday, we're also investigating the use of DLC- servers in the future, so maybe later (or maybe during ISC HPC
conference) we could meet if we have questions.

Thanks and have a good weekend!

Best regards,
Maarten

issues.description:
Hi Arthur,

I hope you are well. 

I am experiencing a potential issue with a G294-S41-AAP2 system.

Every 30 - 50 hours of BMC uptime, the BMC appears to reboot itself. I’m wondering if this is normal behaviour for this system.

The BMC does not completely crash, but it seems to restart unexpectedly. For example, yesterday I checked the BMC uptime under the System Information section, and it was reporting 1d 18hrs 22mins. Today, when I logged back in, the uptime had reset to 0d 13hrs 20mins. The system itself does not crash or reboot, just the BMC, which I am able to regain connection again after a couple of minutes.

I have also tried downgrading both the BMC firmware and BIOS to see if the behavior changes, but the issue still occurs. This makes me wonder if this is expected behavior rather than a hardware fault.

BIOS Version: F23

BMC Firmware Version: 13.06.24

Thanks in advance!


Kind Regards
 
Thomas Bird

notes.text:
Dear Thomas,

There has been an internal job transfer within our team recently.
As part of this transition, Brent will be your new point of contact for all future inquiries related to Gigabyte servers.

It has been a pleasure working with you, thank you for your continued partnership.

Thanks!


Dear Brent,

Please kindly assist with this issue.

Thanks!


Best Regards,
Arthur Wang
Mail:arthur.wang@gigacomputing.com

Dear Thomas, 

Thank you for reaching out. This is Brent from Giga Computing. I’m here to help!

To ensure that I understand the issue correctly, I summarize the situation as below:
You notice that the BMC uptime indicates the BMC has reboot unexpectedly. This does not affect the system OS and you are able to regain the connection after a couple of minutes. And the issue persists with BIOS and BMC FW downgrade. Current FW version is:
-	BIOS Version: F23
-	BMC Firmware Version: 13.06.24

If I understand the situation correctly above, there are some information I would like to collect from you below:
1.	May I know whether this G294-S41-AAP2 is the only system that has this issue?
2.	Did you notice the same behavior when you received the G294-S41-AAP2 system?
3.	If the situation happens a while after you received the system, did you notice anything changed that might be related to the issue?
4.	Could you help to collect the BMC event log with us to check it further?

Note:
I also created a EIP ticket to track this issue:
https://support.gigacomputing.com/issue/details/7802

Let me know if you have any questions or concerns.

I’m looking forward to hearing from you.

Best regards, 
Brent Lin

Hi Brent,

Your summary of the situation is correct. Please see answers to your questions below:

May I know whether this G294-S41-AAP2 is the only system that has this issue?
This is the only system I have with me at the moment. This is the first time I have experienced this issue with a Gigabyte system.

Did you notice the same behavior when you received the G294-S41-AAP2 system?
This was a return from a customer, I am not 100% sure if they had this issue when they received the system - they sent the system back to me for diagnosis.

If the situation happens a while after you received the system, did you notice anything changed that might be related to the issue?
From my understanding, nothing major was changed since this system was shipped out to the customer.

Could you help to collect the BMC event log with us to check it further?
I can send this over, but our company have restrictions in place with what I can and cannot attach to an email to an external person, so this may not be possible. From what I can see in the BMC event logs though, nothing major was occurring, when the BMC did reboot itself, it was not logged into the BMC.

I was mainly wondering whether this behaviour is normal, and whether you have seen anything similar (or the same) before - specifically, if this indicates an actual fault or if this is simply how the Gigabyte BMC is designed to operate.

I have already tried numerous troubleshooting steps, but they have all led to dead ends, including:
•	Minimal configuration
•	Swapping CPUs
•	Swapping RAM
•	Booting with only 1 CPU and 1 RAM stick
•	Downgrading BIOS and BMC FW as previously mentioned

Thank you in advance and I look forward to hearing back from you.


Kind Regards
 
Thomas Bird

No similar case found on G294 for BMC reboot issue. 

the only G294-S41-AA2P ticket I can find is:
https://support.gigacomputing.com/issue/details/7171

Hi Thomas, 

Thank you for your reply in sharing the current situation with us. 

Based on the fact that the host OS system is stable and nothing major was shown in BMC event log, my judgement is that this is a normal case. I also searched our ticket system and I don’t see a similar case happened on G294. 

Since you mentioned that you are checking this system from your customer for diagnosis, may I confirm that whether your customer ship the system to you because of the BMC reboot issue?

Best regards, 
Brent Lin

Hi Brent,

Thank you for looking into this for me.

In response to your question, yes - the customer returned this system to us due to BMC issues. In their case, the only way to restore the BMC is to fully power cycle the system by unplugging the power cables and reconnecting them.

However, during my diagnosis (as mentioned), the BMC comes back online on its own after a few minutes of being unresponsive.

If you can confirm that this is expected/normal behavior, I can relay this to the customer and see what feedback they provide.

Thanks again!


Kind Regards
 
Thomas Bird

Hi Brent,

Please include me in any related responses. 

This is a Broadberry customer and they're not happy with how long it is taking us to work out why the BMC causes the machine to crash after approximately 30 hours for no apparent reason. 

If I tell them, it is normal, I doubt it will go over very well. �

As always, your assistance is appreciated. 

Kind Regards
 
Bryn Downing
General Manager
Broadberry Data Systems

Hi Thomas/Bryn, 

Thank you for sharing the customer feedback with us. 

Yes, I agree that the behavior you observed is different from the customer’s feedback. 
-	In your case (Thomas), it is expected that BMC should be able to recover the access from reboot in few minutes. 
-	In the customer’s case, it is not an expected case that the BMC needs to have an AC cycle to recover the access. 

If I understand it correctly, we couldn’t reproduce the customer’s issue on Thomas side so far. If this is the case, we may need to clarify the differences between these two tests that what triggers the issue. 

Here is the information I would like to gather to understand the issue:
1.	BMC event log: Could you please advise if you have a different method to share the BMC event log with us?
2.	This ticket is originally discussed about the BMC uptime, but from the new information we gather so far, the machine crashed caused by BMC. Could you elaborate it further about:
a.	What is the machine crashed symptom? 
b.	May I know how did you identify the machine crashed is caused by BMC?
c.	What was the system uptime (not BMC uptime) before machine crash?
d.	When the system is crashed, were the customer able to access BMC?

Please feel free to share further information with us to understand the situation further. 

Best regards, 
Brent Lin

Hi Brent,

The system itself does not crash as a result of the BMC behaviour.

The original issue reported by the customer was that approximately every 30-31 hours, the BMC becomes unreachable. The only way for them to regain access is to completely remove AC power and reconnect it - performing a full power cycle.

During my testing, I observed that the BMC remains up anywhere between 30-50 hours. After that period, the BMC appears to reboot; however, I am able to regain access without performing a full power cycle, and the operating system remains stable throughout. I have been continuously monitoring the system within Windows, and there have been no OS crashes.

I determined that the BMC is rebooting because the reported uptime resets. For example, one day the BMC system information may show an uptime of 1 day, 15 hours, and 6 minutes. The following day, it may show 13 hours and 4 minutes. This indicates that the BMC rebooted itself during that time. Despite this, I am able to log back in immediately by refreshing the browser.

There are no entries in the BMC logs indicating hardware or software faults. After each BMC reboot, no new or concerning log entries are generated.

Given the lack of log errors and the fact that the OS remains stable, I am trying to determine whether this behavior is expected or if there is an underlying fault. I would appreciate any clarification or additional troubleshooting steps you would recommend, as I feel I have exhausted the available testing options at this stage.

I can try get the logs over to you but it may have to be done via gofile if this is possible your end.


Kind Regards
 
Thomas Bird

Hi Thomas, 

Thank you for sharing the situation with us.

This is the current high-level summary based on the current situation:
1.	The customer can reproduce the issue In every 30 hours. The symptoms is BMC becomes unreachable, and only AC cycle can recover the access. 
2.	In your side, so far, you have not observed the issue. Because you don't need to do the AC cycle to regain the BMC access. 
3.	OS stays stable in both environments. 

If I understand it correctly above, I suggest clarifying the symptoms that the customer said they couldn't access BMC. For example:
1.	Was the customer able to access BMC GUI via Ethernet cable?
2.	Was the customer able to access BMC from OS via IPMI tool? 
By collecting this kind of information, We would be able to clarify if the BMC is available via ethernet cable or via OS. 

In another hand, I would like to set the expectation that the progress would be limited if the issue is not reproducible anymore. 

Regarding the BMC event log, yes, please share the logs via gofile with us. Could you also share the timeline about when the customer started to experience the issue, and when the customer ship the server to you? I am collecting this information to ensure the time frame that I should focus on. 

Best regards, 
Brent Lin

Hi Brent,

Your summary of the situation is now correct.

Additional information from the customer: the system itself was accessible via SSH; however, the BMC was not reachable. They attempted to access the BMC GUI using an Ethernet cable connected to the dedicated BMC port. They did not attempt to access the BMC from the operating system using the IPMI tool.

I have uploaded the SEL logs to WeTransfer, as there appears to be an issue with GoFile at the moment. Please see the link below:

https://we.tl/t-PwGQbq8QN2

It appears the customer first experienced issues with the BMC around the beginning of December, and we received the unit at the beginning of January.


Kind Regards
 
Thomas Bird

Time is obviously against us given when the machine was received guys.

Anything we can do to bring about a quick resolution would be greatly appreciated. 

Kind Regards
 
Bryn Downing
General Manager
Broadberry Data Systems

Hi Bryn,

I understand the urgency of this case and am committed to resolving it as quickly as possible.

However, since:
1.	This is the single case in the customer side. 
2.	The issue cannot be reproduced in Thomas side. 
3.	We do not have a direct line of communication with the end user. 

We must rely on your assistance to gather more specific details from their side. To move the investigation forward and avoid further delays caused by the inability to reproduce the error, we need to confirm the following with the end user:

1.	Reproduction Steps: What are the exact steps or specific workloads required to trigger this issue? Knowing the precise environment and sequence of events is crucial for us to replicate the fault in your lab. (ex: network setting of BMC, or network environment from client side to BMC…etc)
2.	BMC Access via IPMI: When the issue occurs, is the end user able to access the BMC directly from OS using the IPMI tool?

Without being able to reproduce the issue locally, our ability to narrow down the issue and discuss a resolution is highly limited. Having these details will allow us to move closer to the root cause.

Please let me know if you like to set up a quick meeting that includes you, me, Thomas, and the customer to ensure we are on the same page. 

Best regards,
Brent Lin

Hi Brent,

Setting up a joint call may be difficult at this point. The customer is already unhappy with how long this has taken and now that the system is with us, they are no longer able to test BMC access from the OS using the IPMI tool on their side. Because of that involving them in a live session may not be productive right now.

It may be possible for Bryn to gather the additional reproduction details from the customer, but given the current situation I am not sure how well that will be received. At this stage he is mainly looking for a clear update confirming that his system is stable and working.

Are there any additional logs, debug options or tools you would recommend we enable to help move this forward even if we cannot fully reproduce it yet?


Kind Regards
 
Thomas Bird

Hi Thomas, 

Thank you for the candid update. I completely understand the sensitivity of the situation, and we certainly want to avoid adding any further frustration for the customer.

While it’s difficult to capture logs without a reproduction, here is how we can consider proceeding:

1.	System Stability monitoring: Based on your internal testing, the system has been stable, and we have not encountered any BMC access issues. We can continue to monitor the behavior to ensure everything remains operational.
2.	Networking environment: Since we cannot reproduce the issue in your current environment, I’m suspecting the issue is highly likely tied to specific network configurations or environmental factors at the customer's site.

Let me know if you like to have a call with me for any questions or concerns. 

Best regards, 
Brent Lin

Hi Brent,

From my understanding of your reply, are you suggesting that this is normal behaviour? I'm not trying to put words in your mouth - I just want to ensure I fully understand the current situation and your recommendation so I know how best to proceed with the next steps.

If you do believe this is normal, could you please provide some clarification on why you feel that way?

As Bryn mentioned earlier in this email chain, we can’t simply go back to the customer and say that this is normal without a clear explanation. I hope you understand our position on this.

While I can continue to monitor the system, we are losing valuable time by waiting for it to "fail" again or by continuing to observe it, especially as this is something I have already been doing over the past few weeks.


Kind Regards
 
Thomas Bird

Hi Knut, 

抱歉我的前一封信沒有表達清楚，依照客戶的圖片看起來，BMC確實有reboot:

你擷取的圖片是客戶看到的BMC Long Power-On Hours.png， 客戶表示這是他在這台機器上觀察到最長的BMC 開機時間。
 

如果我們看第二張Current Timings.png的話，可以看出BMC開機時間從四天變成一天:
 

我引用客戶的說法，並且整理時間順序如下:
1.	在系統關機的情況下，觀察BMC開機時間。
2.	系統開機半小時左右，擷取Current Timings.png，客戶表示這是他觀測到最長的BMC開機時間(4 d 4 hrs)。
3.	一天過後，客戶發現OS系統還在，但是BMC的時間從四天變成一天。
4.	客戶收集BMC event log from GUI and then collect the system log from gbtipmitool. 

客戶的說法:
…While we were having this email conversation, the system was powered off but still connected to power. During that time, I logged into the IPMI and noticed that the "Power-On Hours" showed 4d 4hrs, which is the longest duration I have seen reported. The "System power on time" showed only 29 minutes, as I had just powered the system on.

Afterward, while waiting for the BMC to crash as previously mentioned, I observed that both timers reset at the moment the crash occurred.

This suggests that the BMC only "crashes" when the system is powered on.

At present, both "Power-On Hours" and "System power on time" are showing 1d 2hrs and 1d 2hrs 39mins, respectively….


如果有任何我可以和客戶確認的現象都可以隨時和我說，感謝。

Best regards, 
Brent

issues.description:
Summary :
all R263-Z37-AAH1 (intended to be converted into AAH2) are delivered with default fan profile set to “SPECpower” instead of the “default” fan profile
Description :
As the minimum fan speed is set to 10% in SPECpower profile (versus 30% in the “default” profile) it cause the OCP card in Slot 7 to overheat within minutes.
Every time we need to reset to default the BMC or we need to flash the BMC (causing it to be reset to default), it cause the BMC to switch to the “SPECpower” fan profile and the card overheat. we already have 3 cards damaged.
Please provide us a way to switch to the correct fan profile in a permanent way and explain why we have such fan profile as default that seems really non functional at all

Firmware:
BIOS Version: 13.06.16
BMC Version: R25_F42
CMC Version:

We would like to ask how the OCP card overtemperature was determined this time.

Based on the normal system behavior, during system power-on (before the POST phase), all fans will run at full speed, and fan RPM will not be reported in either the Web UI or ipmitool. 
After the system boots, control is handed over to the BMC, and the fan profile switches to the “default” mode. Under this behavior, an overtemperature condition should no longer occur.

Therefore, we would like to ask you to share how it was concluded that the OCP card is still overheating. Was it because the card was not detected when entering the OS?

Hello Daniel

my constatation is that at power on, the fans indeed runs at full speed but shortly after power on (about 1-2 minutes), BMC starts to regulate (and therefore goes to idle speed) while the host is still POST'ing.
Regulation starts way before the end of POST (POST take about 7 minutes)
When POST finishs and the BMC finally see the OCP card , it's too late, OCP card already overheated , even using "default" fan profile if the ambiant temperature is above 25C
when using the "SpecPower" fan profile the situation is even worse as, at 10% idle speed the OCP card will literally fries
using "Default" fan profile , we just have a temporary overheat

My opinion is that the BMC starts to regulate too early based on incomplete sensors.

best regards
-- 
Thomas

Hello Daniel,

I realise that I didn't answered the question in "we would like to ask you to share how it was concluded that the OCP card  is still overheating"

We see it because when the host finishs POST, the OCP card temperature appears (SLOT7_TEMP sensor) beyond 100 degrees, the event logs reports temperature alerts , and the Mellanox driver in the host reports it also

Best regards

Thomas

Customer Scaleway (EU) Issue ID 7074

* Problem: R263-Z37 servers (intended as AAH2) defaulted to “SPECpower” fan profile instead of “default”, causing OCP card overheating in Slot 7. The issue persisted after BIOS/BMC upgrades. The “SPECpower” profile’s low minimum fan speed (10% vs 30% in “default”) was the root cause. Initial BMC regulation occurred *before* full POST completion & OCP card detection. New SKU (20260130) + BMC 13.06.22 resolves overheating. Existing servers need the SKU/BMC update. Issue also observed on R263-Z36.
* Next Action: Apply SKU 20260130 + BMC 13.06.22 to all existing servers. Confirm future shipments use this combination.
* LastActivityFromCreation: 97 days
* Status: assigned

issues.description:
Hello support team,

I desperately try to update the BIOS of one server of our h374 chassis and it's not working.
The current BIOS is F10 (servers were delivered with it), I try to flash F20.

At the beginning I used 2 CPU 6980P with full MRDIMM: after the BIOS flash, the start of the server always stops at "memory initialisation" with an ECC error on slot A. DIMMs are new so I really doubt it's the problem.

I removed 1 CPU and remove all the DIMMs, I used only one DIMM of DDR5 6400 in slot A: still "memory initialisation" with an ECC error but now on slot B: there is no DIMM in slot B.

1) How an ECC error can be seen on an empty slot ? I think there is a bug here, it's not the first time I see this kind of behaviour on GB servers.
2) How can I update the BIOS ?
3) Regularly the power commands are not working (power on/off, reset of the server) booth with gbtipmitool or in the web interface. Cold reset of the BMC may bring back the control. I guess this is a bug in the 13.06.18.

Thank you.

Firmware:
BIOS Version: F10
BMC Version: 13.06.18
CMC Version:

reply:SSH/BMC shell access is disabled by design in GCT BMC firmware due to security and system integrity reasons.
May I know what kind of information you want to read from the FPGA board?

from customer : Actually, we are using an FPGA board of our own design. It contains a CPLD Max10 + Agilex7 from Altera, and we have i2c bus interface available as sideband on pcie edge connector that we’d like to drive from BMC. This i2cset/get/dump commands are sent to Max10 to drive our design in Agilex (for example, to reset/power_on/off the design in Agilex by accessing Config Reg in Max10). This is the main reason why we need to run this kind of commands through BMC with ssh connection with I2C set of commands …

Is there another way to do the same with current configuration of BMC on our genoa based systems ?

To BMC:
Eviden使用的是他們自己設計的FPGA卡，希望透過BMC來驅動它
這張卡上有:
•	Intel Max10(CPLD)
•	Intel Agilex 7(FPGA)
在 PCIe edge connector 上有 sideband 的 I²C bus，這條 I²C 是接給 BMC 的，不是 OS 端

目前的控制流程是:
BMC 透過 I2C 指令（i2cset / i2cget / i2cdump） > 去存取 Max10 的暫存器 > 再由 Max10 控制 Agilex

客人現在遇到的問題是 : 目前的 BMC不允許SSH 下 i2c-tools，導致 無法控制 FPGA 板卡
因此客人想知道 : 
在不開放 raw i2c-tools 的情況下，有沒有其他 supported 的方式，可以讓 BMC 去操作這條 I²C?

客戶原文:
Actually, we are using an FPGA board of our own design. It contains a CPLD Max10 + Agilex7 from Altera, and we have i2c bus interface available as sideband on pcie edge connector that we’d like to drive from BMC. This i2cset/get/dump commands are sent to Max10 to drive our design in Agilex (for example, to reset/power_on/off the design in Agilex by accessing Config Reg in Max10). This is the main reason why we need to run this kind of commands through BMC with ssh connection with I2C set of commands … Is there another way to do the same with current configuration of BMC on our genoa based systems ?

請問這需求使否需要SWGSI?

BMC
SSH因為安全因素不開放, 但可以透過ipmi master write/read達到目的。
PJ
先問一下客戶device接到BMC的I2C bus實際路徑，中間是否有I2C Mux chip，我們協助提供範例用的IPMI command

We would like to know the actual I²C bus path used to connect the device to the BMC, and whether there is any I²C multiplexer (I²C Mux) chip in between. 
Based on this information, we can assist by providing example IPMI commands for reference.

客人使用” i2cdetect” 來掃描設備，尋找對address 0xa 有回應的裝置。客人使用的 FPGA 板子，可以在 /dev/i2c-8 或 /dev/i2c-10 的偵測到這個address。然後，客人可以透過命令 i2cset|get -y 8 (或 10) 0xa reg_addr [value] 訪問 0xa

以下是客戶原文:
Normally, we try to use ‘i2cdetect’ to look up devices, searching for the one which responds to address 0xa. Usually, according to FPGA board we use, we can detect this address on bus /dev/i2c-8 or 10. Then, we access registers of device 0xa through “i2cset|get -y 8 (or 10) 0xa reg_addr [value]”

請問以上資訊是否足夠我們協助客戶?

Sorry we have no system(card) to verify it, but you can refer to the document below to check.

SLOT3(MB_SLOT2) I2C path:
BMC[1]/I2C[1] -> 0xE6(9548_1) -> 0xE0(9548_0) -> PCIE_SLOT1 。
SLOT4(MB_SLOT3) I2C path:
BMC[1]/I2C[1] -> 0xE6(9548_2) -> 0xE0(9548_0) -> PCIE_SLOT1 。
SLOT6(MB_SLOT1) I2C path:
BMC[1]/I2C[1] -> 0xE6(9548_0) -> 0xE0(9548_1) -> PCIE_SLOT2 。
SLOT11(MB_SLOT4) I2C path:
BMC[1]/I2C[1] -> 0xE6(9548_3) -> 0xE0(9548_0) -> PCIE_SLOT1 。

issues.summary: Mellanox ConnectX-6 Dx adapter shows pcie link speed degraded from 16Gts to 2.5/5/8Gts in R263
issues.description:
Sorry for the wide audience, as we are not sure who we need to contact for this

We are currently installing all the R263s we bought and we are facing an issue.
The Mellanox ConnectX-6 Dx OCP3.0 network adapter we are using
randomly trains to 2.5 or 8GT/s instead of the expected 16GT/s

a simple reboot solves the issue - sometimes we need to reboot 2-3
times to finally link at 16GT/s

Test conditions :

- Any R263 servers (GBT R263-Z36, R263-Z37 both with motherboard
MZ33-DC1-000) running BIOS R28_F42 and BMC 13.06.17
- Mellanox Cx6 Dx OCP3.0 MCX623436AN-CDAB (MT_0000000327) running
22.46.1006 or 22.46.3048 firmware

Let me know if this is a known issue and if any datas are needed.



System:
Model: R263-Z36/R263-Z37
SN: NA
Processor: 
Memory: 
New Assembled: No
Work Before: No

Firmware:

BMC Version: NA
CMC Version:

notes.text:
How do you confirm the speed is 8GT/s or 16 GT/s?
Could you please help to let me know which OS and version you are using?

We have BIOS version later than our website, please help to check it.
 MZ33-DC0_R29_F43_Web.zip

Could you please help gather the following information from one affected system?
1.	Run the following command twice (once when the link trains at 8GT/s and once when it trains at 16GT/s):
2.	lspci -vvv -s $(lspci | grep Mellanox | awk '{print $1}') > /tmp/mellanox_pcie_log.txt
3.	Please help to dump the system log:
4.	dmesg | grep -i pci > /tmp/pci_dmesg.txt
5.	Please help to provide us SEL.(dump from BMC webui)
6.	Does the issue occur more often after a cold boot or warm reboot?
7.	please help me to check the MB revision.
checking with BIOS

check with customer if any update. 
Also, check:
Could you please clarify whether the CX6 link speed drops from 16 GT/s to 8 GT/s under stress testing, or does it also occur when the system is idle? Do you have specific usage scenarios when the issue happens?

10/30 from customer
The card links randomly at boot at 16/GT/s (correct) , 8GT/s (not correct) , or 2.5GT/s (not correct). the issue does not occurs once the system is booted
I did not see any difference between cold and warm boot but my sampling is a bit low.
 
the fact that I know for sure is that I do not see the issue in another manufacturer machine with a AMD Siena CPU , I did not have the chance to try on another AMD Turin server
 
I did some extensive testing and see the issue in BIOS R25, R28 and R29 with no différences
I also tried 13.06.18, 13.06.17 and 13.06.xx
I tried Mellanox f/w 22.46.3048 , 22.45.1020 and 22.46.1006
I tried various linux kernel also to be sure that there is no influence ,we are using Ubuntu 20.04 with kernel 5.8.0 , ubuntu 24.04 with Kernel 6.8.0 and Ubuntu 24.04 with kernel 6.15.11
 
I can also reproduce the issue once the server is booted by issuing a bus reset , the card will link randomly at 2.5/8/16 GT/s
We did not have the chance to test with another network card , I will do it today.
 
I believe that the issue is easily reproducible , did you manage to reproduce it in your labs ?
 
I Wwill come back to you with the datas asap.

10/31 from customer

whether the CX6 link speed drops from 16 GT/s to 8 GT/s under stress testing, or does it occur when the system is idle?
→how long will it take to see the degrade in idle status? Or it will be triggered under system stress? Which stress tool you’re using?

The incorrect speed is immediate , no need to stress the server , just booting or rebooting the server is sufficient.

Which CPU and DIMM are using on the system?
 
I see it on any systems namely :

9555P + 8 x M321R8GA0EB2-CCPWF
9555P + 12 x M321RAJA0MB2-CCPPC
9355P + 8 x M321R4GA3EB2-CCPPF
9355P + 8 x M321R8GA0EB2-CCPWF
9135 + 8 x M321R4GA3EB2-CCPK

 
And from the description, I have 2 ways to see the issue:
1.	During the system booting, we can see the link speed show 16/GT/s, 8/GT/s, 2.5/GT/s randomly.
2.	I can also reproduce the issue once the server is booted by issuing a bus reset , the card will link randomly at 2.5/8/16 GT/s
→what do you mean issuing a bus reset?

I use a script that set the reset bit and clear it on the parent bus eg :
setpci -s e0:01.1 0x3E.W=40:4
setpci -s e0:01.1 0x3E.W=0:40

but anycase just rebooting the server a few time will trigger the issue/

Please find enclosed the data requested in 2 archives , one when the link is correct , one when the link is at 2.5GT/s

The server is a r263-z36-aav1 s/n GPG152821A0004
System board is a MZ33-DC1-000 s/n OHCN8700079

客戶卡:MCX623436AN-CDAB Gen4 x16 Dual port
我們卡:MCX631432AS-ADAB Gen4 x8 Dual port
QVL:MCX565M-CDAB Gen4 x16 Single port

Rev 1.2之前的版本有一顆電阻有異常，開機容易頂到ocp，所以會隨機造成掉速，有中就是把把中，也有沒看到的時候。
所以我需要整批的sn去查mb是不是1.2前的
另外兩個可能性”
1.卡過熱，idle狀況還會這樣，就是風扇沒轉，可能是沒偵測到卡，就要問bmc。客人那邊請他們看sensor list下的ocp sensor還有試試風扇全轉有無改善。

For AMD platform:
AMD CBS -> NBIO Common Options -> Link EQ Preset Options > Link EQ Preset Options > GEN4 > Preset Search Mask (Gen4) > We would like to ask for your assistance in contacting NVIDIA to clarify whether the following two settings have the same definition: "Gen3 Transmitter Preset / Gen4 Transmitter Preset set to 5" and the CBS item.

Hello Daniel

here is the update for Nvidia verbatim :

we checked the previous dump log and got current gen3/4 preset = 4/4 which means no pre-emphasis
The latest log down speed to gen1 which cannot get the preset value, if you didn't change the tx preset, we would suspect it is preset issue.
Can you set gen3/4 tx preset = 5/5 in the bios?
Depending on the host make it can be in a few places.
Enter the BIOS GUI, select advanced mode->PCIe/PCI/PnP (or PCI Express Configuration) → PCIe Ports or slot → Port /slot # : select the cx6 slot# → Gen3 Transmitter Preset / Gen4 Transmitter Preset → set to 5 → Save & Exit.

Best regards
-- 
Thomas
I'm checking on it internally. will keep you updating.

custom_fields.value:
CRITICAL – (Customer Scaleway (EU)) Issue ID 6921

* Problem: Mellanox ConnectX-6 Dx OCP3.0 network adapters on R263 servers (R263-Z36/Z37) randomly train to 2.5 or 8GT/s instead of the expected 16GT/s. A reboot sometimes resolves the issue, but can require multiple attempts. Initial investigation pointed to potential motherboard revision issues (pre-1.2), firmware bugs, and PCIe settings. Recent testing suggests a temperature-related component, and the issue appears to be reproducible with specific server configurations and potentially linked to the CPU type (Turin vs. Genoa/Siena). The issue was duplicated internally with a Turin CPU.
* Business Impact: Not specified
* LastActivityFromCreation: 130 days
* Status: assigned

HI:Brent
如附件中所提
針對本次 XV23-VC0（單張 RTX PRO 6000） 之特殊需求，
THERMAL 測試已依照既定流程完成，測試結果如下圖所示，結論說明如下：
測試結論：
1.	原始出貨架構於 30°C 環境溫度條件下無法通過 Thermal 測試，不具備支援能力。
2.	若需滿足 30°C 環溫需求，最終出貨規格必須進行結構調整，
包含 下方 1～3 SLOT 檔板更換為封孔檔板，並加裝導風罩。
未導入上述對策前，不建議宣稱或承諾可支援 30°C 環溫條件。
3.	依目前實測結果，僅在導入上述改善對策後，系統方可於 30°C 環溫下穩定運作。
 

 

BL.Wang 王百祿
熱流工程師

Hi 百祿 and all,

notes.text:
Hi Gene,

Please follow up with this.

Thanks!

Hi Kenny
I chatted with EE and he gave me something that need customer to help to confirm:

1. Boot up with 16 DIMMs to collect logs, then swap the DIMMs at slot B0 and slot B1 to repeat the test and get the logs again. The more times you test, the more helpful it will be for analysis. This is to check if the issue is from a specific DIMM or related to the slot.

2. Do they have any DIMMs on hand that exactly match the product names on the QVL for testing? (Attached image 螢幕擷取畫面 2026-02-13 172206.jpg) Because EE mentioned that the last four digits of the product name often represent different materials. Even if they look similar, variations in chips or power modules can affect compatibility and performance.

Hi Gene,

i've requested this for you and pending customer's response.

Hi Gene,

Customer was very fast with the request. They only have that RAM stick unfortunately. 

Here are the updated logs with memory switch.

Hi Kenny,
I didn't have a chance to ask EE today, will ask him tomorrow and keep you updated.
Thanks.

Hi Kenny
Seems the issue appears to follow the specific memory module rather than the slot. This time the system reports "ERR: MC1:R0", while previously it showed "ERR: MC1:R1". EE said that this means the memory is faulty and recommended replacing it.

But are they still failing to boot? If yes, could you help ask them to record a video of the boot process so we can see where it gets stuck? The latest log does not provide enough clarity on this.

Hi Gene,

Asked customer to verify with a video and they cannot obtain a video: 
below is customer's response 
___________________________________________________________________________
Even after multiple reboots and fully repopulating the original system with the same memory modules, it now boots without any errors.

Previously, the same system was fully populated twice with the same memory, and the training error appeared.
On the third attempt, we repopulated the system with the same memory and began collecting logs for you:

Booted with 2 memory modules (per the population table) – no errors
Booted with 4 modules (per the population table) – no errors
Booted with 8 modules (per the population table) – no errors
Then:

Booted the system third time with all 16 DIMMs installed in the original configuration (SOL logs collected) – the training error appeared again
Swapped the DIMM in slot B0 with the DIMM in slot B1 (SOL logs collected) – the error no longer occurred; the system booted successfully with all memory modules
Performed 5 consecutive reboots – no errors
Moved all memory modules to three other systems – no errors
Reinstalled the same memory modules back into the original system – booted successfully on the first attempt, no errors
Since then, we have not been able to reproduce the memory training error on the original system.
_______________________________________________________________________________________________________
^ Above is what troubleshooting has been done and customer claims cannot replicate issue. 
Have you seen this problem before?

Hi Gene,

Customer updated they replicated issue again. 
Sending their response below: and also dropping their logs attachments
___________________________________________
When the system is fully populated with all 16 DIMMs, it does not consistently detect all memory modules during POST when BIOS memory is set to AUTO.

For comparison, we have a second system from the same order with identical configuration and memory. That system consistently boots with BIOS set to AUTO and detects all 16 DIMMs without issue.

Test Summary (Problematic System)
Boot 1–3: BIOS set to AUTO → Not all memory detected
Boot 4–6: BIOS set to 5600 → All memory detected
Boot 7: BIOS set back to AUTO → Not all memory detected
 
Additional observation:
Yesterday, without performing a full power pull (only reboot and OFF/ON), the system failed to detect all memory modules even when set to 5600.
Today, after performing a full power pull, the system successfully booted at 5600 with all 16 DIMMs detected.
Attaching:
1. Boot SOL logs from all seven test cycles on the affected system
2. Five SOL boot logs from the second (known-good) system, which consistently detects all 16 DIMMs with BIOS set to AUTO

notes.text:
Customer emailed with another update: 
The same set of Micron 128GB with PN# MTC40F2047S1RC64BB1R is working at 7 systems, but not 1.  We are using the latest F12 BIOS revision on all of them.  

For the 4 systems from 2025, when insert the Micron 128GB with PN# MTC40F2047S1RC64BB1R, they boot normally, but not with Samsung 64GB PN# M321R8GA0PB0.  But 6 systems at the same 2025 order, they boot normally with Samsung 64GB PN# M321R8GA0PB0 and they were all using the F11 BIOS.  To troubleshoot, we also have tried BIOS version F10 or upgrade to F12 with same memory training error.  

We would like to emphasize.  The same (Samsung or Micron) memory module ONLY work on some systems, but not all.  Please let us know if there are any question.  Thank you.

Hi Kenny,
We're a bit confused about what customer replied and logs they provided, so I have a few points that would like to check: 
1. All these the latest logs are from the same system? Or different systems?
2. Are they testing by using the same 16 pieces DIMM?
3. Manually set up memory speed to 5600 is working normally? But all memory can not be detected if set to AUTO?

And below are suggestions,
1. Try to set memory speed to 4400 manually, then see if everything work properly on every systems in every tests. 
    The limitation of AmpereOne CPU is that, support to 5600 when 1DPC, but 2DPC can not. (Please refer to the attached image_01 and image_02.)
2. According to the logs, please suggest the customer to switch the DIMM on G0 and G1 these two slots. The issue should follow the DIMM. (Please refer to the attached image_03 and image_04.)
3. Are they able to check if the CPU is properly locked? Or could they switch and check if this issue is caused by this specific CPU?

To sum up, EE believe this issue is from the DIMM so please request the customer try the suggestions above and see if there is any result to share with us.

Hi Andy
Attached are the screenshot from EE.

According to the limitation of the Ampere CPU, Ampere CPUs require strict following to their official QVL for memory compatibility. To guarantee stability, the memory part numbers must match the QVL exactly, character by character. However, our EE team has provided a cross-validation method to verify if the issue follows the DIMM or the motherboard:

1. The error logs point to MCU4 and MCU6 (Slots G0, G1, E0, E1). To isolate the root cause, please tag these DIMMs and relocate them to different slots, such as A0, A1, B0, and B1, for further observation.

If the error follows these four DIMMs, it confirms a compatibility issue. Then if the customer have any concerns regarding this conclusion, we suggest they consult directly with their Ampere representative for further verification.

Channel (USA) Issue ID 7784

* Problem: Customer reports 4 servers fail to boot with 16x 128GB DIMMs (MTC40F2047S1RC64BB1). System boots with 8 DIMMs. Initial testing showed errors followed specific DIMM, suggesting a faulty module. However, issue is intermittent – sometimes boots, sometimes doesn't. Testing with 4400MHz shows only 2 of 16 DIMMs enabled, with numerous training errors reported in SOL logs ("WRDSK Window too small", "PDA Write unsuccessful"). The same Micron DIMMs work in 7 other systems, but not this one. Samsung DIMMs exhibit similar inconsistent behavior depending on the system and BIOS version.
* Next Action: Investigate CPU locking. Request customer to re-test with CPU swapped. Further analysis of SOL logs needed to understand training failures at 4400MHz.

issues.description:
AMD (Michael @ AMD) has advised that this be formally reported to Gigacomputing for review.

Customer Reported Issue:

The customer deployed 10x AMD EPYC 9965 CPUs in R283-ZF1-AAL1 Rev.3 systems delivered in July 2025.
After approximately six months of operation, two systems began exhibiting the following POST error:
“Chipset Initialization error. BIOS does not retain CPU related settings.”

Troubleshooting Performed:

The customer performed cross-testing to isolate the issue:
- Installed the suspected CPU into a known-good R283-ZF1-AAL1 Rev.3 barebone
- The same error message was reproduced
- No other component faults were identified during testing
Based on their isolation testing, the customer believes the CPUs may be defective.

Concern:

With a 20% observed failure rate from this shipment batch, the customer is concerned about potential additional failures in the near future. The current observed time-to-failure is approximately 6 months.

Request for HQ Support:

- Engineering review of the reported failures
- Guidance on further diagnostics or log collection required
- Recommendation on whether this may be batch-related
- Next steps for coordination with AMD

Please review for ATIPA.

issues.description:
System Information:

Product: R283-Z92-AAE3
Serial Number: GNGERRV12A0022
Symptom: Amber SYS light on boot, MCA error (Processor error) in IPMI webGUI
 

Troubleshooting Performed: We have conducted extensive troubleshooting to isolate the root cause:

Swapped CPU positions between socket 1 and socket 2 - error persisted
Tested with single CPU configuration in socket 1 - no error
Installed CPU in socket 2 only - error appeared
Verified both CPUs in a known-good system - both CPUs function normally in both sockets
Installed known-good CPUs from working system into suspect motherboard:
CPU in socket 1 only: no error
CPU in socket 2: MCA processor error returns
BIOS settings reset and reconfigured multiple times - error persists
Conclusion: The issue is isolated to the CPU2 socket/motherboard hardware. Any CPU installed in socket 2 triggers the MCA processor error, while the same CPUs work perfectly in socket 1 or in another system entirely.

Testing and Environment:
Ambient Room Temperature Under 20C
Burn-in testing in an open environment with no ceilings. All the cold air from roof come down directly to the room. Freezing cold in Kansas.

Customer is looking for a resolution, optional heatsinks for Turin 9845, or alternatives.

issues.summary: GBTGSI - MZB3-G43 - Custom boot logo not displayed on boot
issues.description:
When creating a custom BIOS (R15_F21) in gbtgsi, the boot logo does not show up after "Please wait for chipset initialize..."

How to reproduce:
1. Create custom BIOS for G493-ZB0-AAP1-000 / MZB3-G43 with bootlogo
2. Flash custom BIOS from 1. onto such a system and boot
3. Boot into BIOS setup, make sure "Disable quiet boot" is set to "Enabled"
4. Reboot and observe in KVM

Expected behaviour:
After "Please wait for chipset initialize..." the bootlogo inserted in 1. shows up

Under the current fw policy, 

BIOS
Any BIOS version with a letter suffix after the version number (for example, R17a_F35d) is considered a Beta version.
Beta versions are provided for validation or specific purposes and will not be published on the official website.

BIOS versions without any letter suffix (for example, R18_F07) are considered official releases and will be uploaded to the official website.

BMC
For BMC firmware, we will not add an additional column in the weekly list to indicate whether a specific version will be published on the official website.

This is because all BMC firmware versions included in the weekly release list are already official releases.
If a BMC version appears in the list, it means that the version is intended for general availability and will be published on the official website.

Pre-release, internal, or test BMC firmware versions are not included in the weekly list and are therefore out of scope for customer notification

-Also, if you turn the server off and check the heatsinks, one heatsink is way hotter than the other ones.
-One Customer of ours also has one of these servers with recently the same problem.

issues.summary: MI300A: Mgmt, VGA port and front panel power button dysfunctional after changing BIOS settings
issues.description:
After applying our BIOS settings and rebooting this system, my colleagues were confronted with the following effects:
- Mgmt port LEDs went out
- BMC no longer reachable on previous IP address
- No video output on VGA port
- Unable to power off system via power button

I tried to reproduce this phenomenon and was able to do that by:
(0. Flash BMC and BIOS, boot into OS)
1. Set Boot Order using redfish to:
{
    "FBO201": "UEFI USB Device",
    "FBO202": "UEFI Network",
    "FBO203": "UEFI Hard Disk",
    "FBO204": "UEFI AP",
    "FBO205": "Disabled"
}
2. issue a reboot from within the OS.


The only way to recover was to unplug the system from AC and then reconnect.
After that, the system recovered from the effects described above.
The boot order has been reset to it's defaults:
{
    "FBO201": "UEFI Hard Disk",
    "FBO202": "UEFI CD/DVD",
    "FBO203": "UEFI USB Device",
    "FBO204": "UEFI Network",
    "FBO205": "UEFI AP"

issues.summary: <<260217UK03>> R162-ZA1-A00 — NVMe boot failure after OS install (WD Red NVMe) — request for confirmation + BIOS settings.
issues.description:
Hello Team,

We have had a customer contact us, with the following :-


We’re deploying a Gigabyte R162-ZA1-A00 load balancing server and are running into an NVMe boot issue.
Issue
•	We can complete the OS installation from USB to the NVMe SSDs, but after reboot the system will not boot from the NVMe drives. 
•	Attempting legacy/BIOS boot mode does not resolve it. 
•	Our working hypothesis is either (a) the specific NVMe models are not supported as boot devices on this platform, or (b) a specific UEFI/BIOS configuration is required for NVMe boot on the NVMe bays/backplane we’re using.

Hardware
•	Server: R162-ZA1-A00 
•	Boot drives: WD Red NVMe SSDs

Request
1.	Please confirm whether WD Red NVMe SSDs are supported as boot devices on the R162-ZA1-A00 (including the specific NVMe bay/backplane configuration). 
2.	If supported, please provide the required BIOS/UEFI settings to boot from NVMe on this platform (e.g., UEFI vs CSM/Legacy, NVMe boot/storage options, Secure Boot state, any relevant PCIe/backplane/slot settings). 
3.	If not supported, please recommend supported NVMe boot drive models (or the preferred boot device approach for this platform).


Please advise




Kind regards

Steve Benham

Field Service Engineer | Technical Field Services

For further correspondence please reference ticket number <<260217UK03>> in the subject line.

issues.description:
Hi Team,

We've had a customer get in touch regarding ticket no 1984703, they are seeing the below issue. Please advise if you can assist?

I am using a Gigabyte MC62-G40-00 (BIOS R14) with an AMD Threadripper PRO 3945WX and 4x dual-slot GPUs (MI50, 32GB) installed in physical slots 1, 3, 5, and 7.

I am reporting the following concrete observations:
 1. Link Training Widths: With 4 GPUs installed, the cards in slots 1, 3, 5, and 7 train at reduced widths (x4 or x8) instead of the expected x16.
 2. Bus Detection in OS: lspci shows PCIe root complexes on buses 00, 20, 40, and 60. However, no root complexes or bridges are visible on buses 80, A0, C0, and E0.
 3. Single GPU Performance: When only one GPU is installed in slot 1, it trains at the full x16 Gen4 width correctly.
 4. Memory Status: All 8 DIMM slots are correctly detected (256GB RAM total) and functional.
 5. BIOS Testing: I have toggled settings for Above 4G Decoding, MMIO limits, IOMMU, and ACS, but the available bus ranges in the OS remain limited to 00-7F.

How can the motherboard be configured to initialize all available root complexes so that 4 GPUs can train at x16 width simultaneously or is this related to a completely different issue altogether?

I have also reseated the CPU, checked pins, contacts, etc.
Server was also placed in both vertical and horizontal configurations.

Kind regards

Caitlyn Gillam

Field Service Engineer | Technical Field Services

For further correspondence please reference ticket number <<260224UK0E>> in the subject line.

With kind regards

STORTREC UK

Subject: RE: <<260224UK0E>> Technical Support Question
 
Hi Caitlyn, 
 
Thanks for reaching out. 
 
There are some information I would like to collect from the customer:
  
1.	How many MC62-G40-00 are in this situation? 
2.	How many MC62-G40-00 do they have in their environment? 
3.	Has the same configuration works in their environment in the past? 
4.	If yes, do they notice anything changed that might change the situation? 
5.	Does the behavior the same if they use the default BIOS setting? 
6.	Could they share the system configuration with us? I want to learn what component is installed on MC62-G40-00, especially the power supply (PSU). 
7.	If they only install 2 GPUs, does both GPUs can be trained to x16?

However, what we have received instead is one enclosure containing 4 nodes, whose serial numbers do not match BSC records and were not expected for this project. Based on this, it seems this shipment may have been sent by mistake.

In parallel, please note that 4 Gigabyte nodes were recently sent back under RMA due to firmware upgrade issues. The enclosure and 4 nodes received are therefore not linked to that RMA.

2.	Unexpected hardware received – possible option

issues.description:
Hello Kevin,

I finally got the feedback on the BMC issue from evroc that we discussed during our meeting early December.

Can you please check if we can get any resource they can work with or that can give us some sort of feedback on this?

Thanks,

--


- Lacking “fleet mangement” capabilities. BMC does an ok job at managing individual servers however the redfish interface differs depending on which type of hardware it is, for example x86 vs ARM. 

- IPv6 support is severely buggy. Setting an IPv6 default gateway is flaky at best so we rely on IPv6 RA. It is not possible to set the RA option via the GUI, we rely on ipmitool.

- Some data is dynamically updated, some is not. It is not clear which data gets updated for example when hot-swapping hardware. Hot-swapped/added/removed U2 drives are not updated. This is most likely due to it being populated via SMBIOS which in turn is only updated at boot. 

- Power consumption figure data is somewhat slow to update.

- Sometimes a BMC reboot is required to make settings take effect.


 
Oscar Kené
	Senior Network Architect
	 
	okene@evroc.com

	+46(0)73-303 22 23


--

--
Med vänliga hälsningar,

Carl-Joel Järvi 
Sales Manager 

+46 70 752 22 50 
cj@enginit.se

Hi Brent, Arthur

Could we have further check on below BMC request from customer side please?

Thank you

 
Kevin Liu
EMEA Sales Department

T : + 886-2-8912-6699 ext. 3206 
M : + 886-936-657-715
E : Kevinyh.liu@gigacomputing.com

Hi Brent,

Could you please help to check it?

Thanks,
Daniel

Hi Kevin/Daniel, 

No problem. Let me discuss it with Carl further. 


Hi Carl, 

Thanks for reaching out and share the customer feedback with us. There are some questions I would like to clarify with you:

1.	What is the Server model(s) that has this issue?
2.	What is the BMC firmware version the customer are currently use?

Regarding to each case:

- Lacking “fleet mangement” capabilities. BMC does an ok job at managing individual servers however the redfish interface differs depending on which type of hardware it is, for example x86 vs ARM. 
 It seems expected to me that the BMC redfish interface is different between x86 and ARM architectures. To understand the situation further, could you please share some examples regarding the different between x86 and ARM?

- IPv6 support is severely buggy. Setting an IPv6 default gateway is flaky at best so we rely on IPv6 RA. It is not possible to set the RA option via the GUI, we rely on ipmitool.
 Could you please share some more details about the steps to reproduce the buggy IPv6 RA settings?

- Some data is dynamically updated, some is not. It is not clear which data gets updated for example when hot-swapping hardware. Hot-swapped/added/removed U2 drives are not updated. This is most likely due to it being populated via SMBIOS which in turn is only updated at boot. 
 Could you list what data is not dynamically updated? Collecting the list will help us to clarify if it is mainly about the hot-swapped/added/removed U2 drives are not updated. 

- Power consumption figure data is somewhat slow to update.
 Could you elaborate a bit further about the delay duration that the customer observed and how long is the customer expected to see the power consumption date update?

- Sometimes a BMC reboot is required to make settings take effect.
 Could you share what settings are in this situation? 
And could you share the fail rate with us so that we can do the same test and confirm the failure rate is the same or not?

Best regards, 
Brent Lin

Hello Brian,

Thanks a lot for your reply. I will ask the end customer to summarize the information and get back to you with this,

--
Med vänliga hälsningar,

Carl-Joel Järvi 
Sales Manager

BMC also changed MAC from burned-in 10:ff:e0:b2:d3:2f to 9e:a0:41:c1:f8:00 and some third one after 'ipmitool mc reset cold' (mainly with 13.06.17).
BMC never recovered to 10:ff:e0:b2:d3:2f.

Frank has some more background.

Questions:
How to get back to original MAC address?
What is the reason for the change?
Why is the BMC lost after some time?
Where do the Linux kernel messages come from?

I'm stressing the system with HPL. The BMC went away several times. I've also attached the rsyslog information sent by the BMC to the now configured rsyslog server under advanced log settings. The timestamp around 13:28 is when the BMC rebooted the last time.

I think that the inband i2c-designware-driver hangs/crashes and generates kernel messages because the BMC reboots in the background. Normally, we're monitoring the sensor values with ipmitool sdr (not during the latest tests) and then the kernel driver gets stuck.

6.	git clone https://github.com/spack/spack.git ~/spack
7.	spack install hpl
8.	spack load hpl
9.	Get the location of HPL.dat: 
spack location -i hpl
10.	Copy the HPL.dat from the location you want to execute mpirun, for example:
cp ~/spack/opt/spack/linux-zen4/hpl-2.3-7tcydappwyfh73l4am4bhjuzx5ugzx2t/bin/HPL.dat .
11.	Confirm the stress test can be run: 
mpirun -np 2 xhpl
12.	Run the stress test and leave it overnight:
nohup sh -c 'i=1; while true; do \ echo "[$(date)] 第 $i 次執行開始..."; \ mpirun -np 4 xhpl > /dev/null 2>&1; \ echo "[$(date)] 第 $i 次執行結束，狀態碼: $?"; \ i=$((i+1)); \ sleep 1; \ done' > hpl_monitor.log 2>&1 &
13.	Use tail command to check if the test is still running:
tail -f hpl_monitor.log
14.	Download the SEL log to check if BMC has report the throttling events. 
15.	Open another terminal while system running HPL
Use watch -n 60 ipmitool sdr in new terminal.

Thanks

The first stress test during yesterday afternoon, evening and night for about 12 hours 'just' triggered some CPU throttling events but the second test this morning resulted in BMC reboot. Please see attached logs. 
Unfortunately, there is still no definite reproducer as we run a mixture of IO, memory and CPU related stress tests.

The RD analysis results of logs from 1/14 are as follows: After the CPU problem occurred, the BMC failed to read the CPU sensor through MCTP. After a while, ipmimain was restarted, at which point the customer noticed that IPMI was not working. After another period of time, ipmimain was restarted five times without effect, resulting in a timeout, and then the BMC was restarted. During the restarting process of ipmimain and the BMC, temperature and fan speed could not be monitored, which is suspected to be the cause of CPU throttling. Furthermore, during the BMC restart, the OS ipmidriver also displayed "i2c_designware APMC0D0F:00: timeout waiting for bus ready" due to SSIF failure.

Hi Peter,

Thank you for your continued feedback. We understand the frustration caused by the long investigation period and we appreciate your patience.

During our latest hardware inspection, we identified a loose cable connection at the rear side of the front panel (left side). This was specifically affecting the BPB_FAN sensor readings. When running ipmitool sdr list, the BPB_FAN sensors were reporting ns (no reading). After reconnecting the cable properly:

All BPB_FAN sensors returned to normal RPM values

Sensor status changed from ns to ok

Fan monitoring is now functioning as expected

This issue was limited to the fan sensor reporting path and has been resolved.

Separately, the BMC crash behavior observed under sustained stress conditions is being analyzed independently. Based on the latest log analysis, we do not see evidence indicating a fundamental hardware defect at this stage. The current focus remains on firmware-level stability validation of the BMC/IPMI stack under heavy workload conditions.

We are continuing long-duration stress testing (HPL + IPMI monitoring) to ensure robustness before confirming shipment readiness. Our goal is to provide you with a concrete timeline update once the extended validation cycle is completed.

We recognize the impact this delay has had and are prioritizing this issue internally to reach a stable release as soon as possible.

Best regards

Hi Peter,

We had received the analyze report from vendor.

Cut to the point: This sulfide formation is attributed to exposure to environmental or operational conditions, and the abnormal resistance values are concentrated in specific locations.

Based on the analysis results, sulfur was found between the silver layers of the component. Sulfur is not a constituent of the Chip-R product and is therefore considered an external contaminant. The normal appearance of the silver layer should be silver-white, rather than black.
When sulfur is present in the environment or during the manufacturing process, it reacts with silver to form Ag₂S (silver sulfide). Ag₂S (silver sulfide) is the cause of discontinuity in the silver layer and the abnormally high resistance values.

   

Can you help investigate if there's any possibllities that can generate the sulfure ?

Thank you
B.R.Cliff

Customer Megware (EU) Issue ID 5967

* Problem: High failure rate of MZ62-HD0 Rev. 3.0 mainboards exhibiting a CLPD error (no boot/no display). Initial investigation points to a potentially faulty CLPD chip. Multiple boards (at least 7 initially, plus 17 and then 30 more) have been flagged with “C03 COMPONENT BAD”. Vendor analysis confirms sulfide formation (Ag₂S) between silver layers in components, indicating external contamination causing high resistance. Boards from Ningbo production (BOM version 3.0, serial numbers starting with KGBN63) are affected. RMA is swapping boards (Revision 3.0 used for advance swap) and sending defective units (2 initially, plus more) to HQ and a third-party for further analysis.
* Next Action: Continue vendor testing, third-party analysis of defective boards, and investigate the source of sulfur contamination. Track status of nodes stuck in customs. RMA to check new batch of 30 boards for the same issue.
* LastActivityFromCreation: 237 days
* Status: assigned

BIOS差異,

目前出貨都是3.x
