Broadberry Data Systems

Hi Dilop, 

Nice to e-meet you too! Apologize for the delay that I was checking the information internally. 

I have received your request in order to clarify the differences between Rev 1, Rev 2, and Rev 3 of MS73-HB0 and after the internal discussion, we can confirm:

1.	The BIOS version is different between Rev 1 and Rev 3. 
2.	Rev 1 support 4th Gen CPU. 
3.	We don’t have Rev 2. 
4.	Rev 3 support 5th Gen CPU. 
5.	Our current shipment will be Rev 3

Best regards, 
Brent Lin

Hi Brent,

Cool, many thanks for that.

Just to confirm - will Rev.3 support both 4th and 5th generation Xeon processors, or only 5th gen?

Kind Regards

Dilip Ladani
Broadberry Data Systems

Hi Dilip, 

Anytime and Rev.3 will support both 4th and 5th generation Xeon processors. 

Please feel free to let me know if you installed and find it doesn’t support. 

Best regards, 
Brent Lin

4.	Please insert the RAID card on the SLOT 11 and check the test result.

b.	The screenshot of the result of “pci -b” from UEFI shell
c.	The screenshot of the result of “sudo lspci -vvvt” and “sudo lspci -vvv” from Ubuntu OS

Please feel free to let me know if there are questions or concerns you would like to discuss with me regarding this system.

Brent Lin

attachments.filename: Re_ 16 GH nodes unable to boot after power off.....eml

notes.text: Update Severity to Serious as customer have 31 + 16 nodes with similar issue and is requesting for FA.

Here’s the summary of the situation and next steps:
Even though the customer claims they encountered this issue after a sudden power outage, we couldn’t reproduce it on our end (we manually AC-cycled the H223 node to see if we could make the BMC crash).
Since the issue reproduction methods are unclear for both the previous and newly affected systems, I’d like to separate the cases into two categories: 
Case1. BMC is alive, but the system is unable to power on (previous 16 nodes)
Case2. BMC crashed (new 31 nodes)

For Case1:
-	Node captured: 4 units, 1 of which was sent to me for analysis.
-	What we did and confirmed on these 4 nodes: 
1.	Update the FPGA firmware directly with a JTAG programmer can recover the system 
2.	Update FPGA firmware again via webUI can reproduce the issue
-	Conclusion:
Either the ASPEED2600 chip (previous experience) or U244 and U243 components are defective and need replacement
-	Action item: 
Caleb helped replace ASPEED chip but the nodes still failed.
Waiting for U244 and U243 components to arrive at USA RMA center, @steven.huang (黃亮源) BY will help for the replacement

For Case2:
-	Node captured: N/A
-	What we want to confirm:
1.	Whether the nodes’ BMC U-boot is still alive or not. This will need physical access to the nodes for UART cable installation 
2.	If the U-boot of the systems is alive, we can first reflash the BMC firmware to see if the systems’ BMC recovers
Instruction attached
If the U-boot is dead, these systems will need to be recalled for further hardware components analysis (possibly the ASPEED2600 chip or BMC flash roms)
3.	Once the BMC is recovered, we can then figure out the next steps
-	Action items:
For issue reproduction: Equipment for automated AC testing is expected to be available this Thursday. Once it's ready, we will put the node to AC testing. 
For failure analysis: We can either send engineers to the customer site or capture the nodes for physical access.

The cartons for the 31 nodes are expected to arrive before 10/24 at USA RMA center.

Thank you for the update!
For Case 1, customer is asking for an update on failure analysis. May I know how to explain what U244 and U243 components are to customer?

Hi Andy,

Locations U244 and U243 use the same component, SN74AVC4T774RSVR (from Texas Instruments).
This component is a level shifter that converts the voltage from 3.3V to 1.8V.
The BMC SPI's working voltage is 3.3V and the FPGA flash uses 1.8V.
So if these components are malfunctioning, this can cause the FPGA flash to fail (damaged).

Thanks!

Today, there is an update from customer that there were:
16 more nodes no longer booting and still diagnosing (47 down total) 
2 chassis that were no longer providing power, this was confirmed after moving the nodes into other chassis and they were working
5 PSUs modules having issues, still waiting customer for more information on this detail.

BSY confirmed they have boxes for chassis systems and will prepare sending the packaging for materials.

For reference only, attached screenshot is a picture of last email chain requesting customer for more information.

From Kevin's feedback, all faulty nodes now have the symptoms of case1, so we need the local FAE team to help us clarify with the customer whether we still have case2.
We have recalled two faulty chassis and two faulty nodes. The USA RMA team will first do some visual inspection before shipping them back to HQ.
Giga schecduled a pick up on 11/7, the tracking number hasn't been sent out yet.

Already picked up, the estimated delivery date to Giga LA office is 11/12.
Tracking number and shipping website: https://www.saia.com/track, 771182903104.
Waiting for Giga local leaders to approve sending back the faulty systems.
Logistics is working on sending the unit however our carrier did not give us a pick update yet.
Waiting for the shipment ETA

We checked all the field returned systems, including chassis and nodes.
All six power supplies and the two chassis are good, and all four nodes are showing case1 symptoms, BMC alive but no post with UART console error message showing "waiting for FPGA to be ready timeout".
As reflashing the FPGA firmware can recover case1 systems, we are arranging for personnel to go onsite recovering these failed servers. 
We will continue to analyze the root cause.
RMA Team will assist on getting additional nodes with case 2 failures back from customer and arrange to send back to TW. Will update ETA end of this week.

Based on local team's preliminary testing results, all boards appear to be case1 nodes.
No need to send them back.
Please proceed with recovery following the FPGA SOP provided.
For root cause analysis, we've sent the faulty flash to MXIC for further investigation, and the FA report is expected to be delivered in early January.

Additionally, while we are still waiting for the root cause analysis, our EE has found a better short term solution than the previous one.
The previous solution could not survive a reupdate.
But the new one, removing FPGA flash2, can.
We'll conduct further testing to verify this approach, If we have a high level of confidence, we'll request the RMA team to remove flash2 from all returned boards.

Confirmed that the new solution can survive a reupdate.
I'll share details with FAE leader Andy and USA RMA team.

RMA leader Evan helped rework 19 pcs motherboards, and the service team will help with the shipment.
Once these 19 pcs are replaced, the remaining quntity will be 15.

We've finished reworking all faulty nodes on hand.
This gives us a total of 19 units ready for replacement.
Our service team is working with the TACC team on the shipment.

custom_fields.value:
Channel (USA) Issue ID 6801

* Problem: Initial power outage caused 31 nodes to fail (no power/BMC lights). Later, 16 more nodes failed, then another 16, totaling 47+ impacted nodes. Initial analysis split failures into two cases: Case 1 (BMC alive, no POST) and Case 2 (BMC crashed). Case 1 linked to FPGA firmware issues – updating firmware via JTAG recovers systems, potentially due to defective ASPEED2600 chip or U244/U243 components. Case 2 investigation stalled due to lack of captured nodes for UART access. Root cause analysis ongoing with MXIC (flash memory investigation). A firmware solution is being tested. A workaround involving removing FPGA flash2 has been confirmed and 19 motherboards have been reworked and replaced. Discrepancies in node counts are being reconciled with TACC.
* Next Action: Continue FPGA firmware solution testing & deployment. Await MXIC root cause analysis report (early January). Finalize remaining node replacements (currently ~15 outstanding).
* LastActivityFromCreation: 140 days
* Status: assigned

issues.summary: Incorrect Core/Thread Count on EPYC 9965 with SMT Enabled/Disabled on R283‑ZF0‑AAL1
issues.description:
Dear Logan,
Good day!
 
The user has faced an issue with R283-ZF0-AAL1(R10_F26 BIOS, IOMUU enabled in BIOS settings) and EPYC 9965: incorrect q-ty of cores and threads:
1.	For single EPYC 9965: SMT disabled -192 cores and 192 threads (OK), but with enabled  SMT – 120 cores and 240 threads (Fail, must be 192 cores and 384 threads)
2.	For dual EPYC 9965: SMT disabled -240 cores and 240 threads (Fail), and with enabled  SMT – 120 cores and 240 threads (Fail)
The user see the same issue on 2 same servers.
Could you kindly check the issue and recommend BIOS settings or provide BIOS update?

notes.text:
Hi Vyacheslav
Can they check in the OS to see whether the issue is still occurring?
Please provide the log after running the command below.
lscpu > lscpu.log
cat /proc/cpuinfo > cpuinfo.log
dmesg | grep -i -E "cpu|apic|ccx|ccd|offline|error" > dmesg_cpu.log
cat /sys/devices/system/cpu/smt/active > smt_state.log
for i in /sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu*/online; do echo $i: $(cat $i); done > core_online_status.log

Can they try another CPU to check whether the issue is related to the stepping or CCD?

Was the system working normally before and the issue only started recently?
Or is this the first time they have enabled SMT?

Also, please provide the sel log
Thanks
Logan Hunag
Best Regard

Hi Logan!

The user has checked servers in Ubuntu Life and Windows Server 2025, the issue is still occurring. Performance of server is abnormal  low , the user see frizzes of interface. 
In Ubuntu:
SMT OFF: smt/active=0, lscpu -2 sockets, 120 cores per socket, threads per core=1, L3=512 MiB (16 instances).
SMT ON: smt/active=1, lscpu – 2 sockets, 60 cores per socket, threads per core=2, L3=256 MiB (8 instances).
for both settings: dmesg: Brought up 8 nodes, 240 CPUs, CPU online, no "offlines" on OS.

Logs from user attached.

issues.description:
Hello Jerry,

we have a Problem with a G293-Z22_AAP1 System, BIOS (R13_F33) and IPMI (13.06.18) on latest Release. The System is equipped with 4 RTX6000 Ada GPUs. In IPMI the Temperature of each GPU is only detected as 4°C, so the FANs don’t ramp up when the GPUs are under load.
In 2024 we had the Same System with 8x RTX 6000 Ada and with that System everything was fine. GPU Temperature was detected and FANs cooled these GPUs just fine.
Right now we have GPU Temperatures of 80°C on two GPUs which would be OK, but we have over 90°C on the other two GPUs and they start throtteling (stopped the Test when they got down to 250W from 300W).
We have put one GPU in each Riser, always in PCIe Slot 2 because of better cooling with the GPU FAN not being blocked by the Chassis.

Mit freundlichen Grüßen / Kind regards,

2/5
Hi Robert,

1. The BMC version 13.06.24 is currently available on the official website. We suggest updating to this version and testing again.
2. Do you have another server of the same model available? If so, could you please provide the BIOS and BMC firmware versions of the other system for comparison?

2/5
Hi Chuan,

i have updated to 13.06.24 without Problem, not preserving any settings.
Now I can see GPU Temperatures in IPMI for four Slots, they stay at 0°C and 31°C all the time. Please check the attached Screenshots. As you can see, the GPUs are detected in Slot 1,3,5 and 7, Temperatures for some of these Slots are “disabled”. IPMI shows Temperatures for the Slots 2,3,5 and 8 but 2 and 3 stay at 0°C and 5 and 8 stay at 31°C. I was running gpuburn at this time, the GPUs had much higher Temperatures. I stopped the gpuburn after the screenshot to prevent any damage. FANs stayed idle the whole time.
The other System was build in April/May 2024, so we used whatever BIOS and Firmware was available on your Homepage that date. According to our logs, IPMI should be 13.05.09. I couldn’t see the BIOS Version in the Logs, so sorry for that.

2/5

2/6
Hi Robert,

Would it be possible to kindly ask for your assistance with the following tests?

1. Firmware Downgrade Test
We will provide BIOS F15 and BMC 13.05.09 for testing purposes.
Would it be convenient for you to first downgrade the BMC, reboot the system, and then help check whether the GPU temperature issue still occurs?
If the issue persists after downgrading the BMC, we would appreciate it if you could then downgrade the BIOS and repeat the same verification steps.
If the issue still cannot be resolved after reverting to the previously working firmware versions, would it be possible to further assist us with the second test below?
  FW

2. GPU Slot Swap Test
Would it be convenient to swap the GPUs between Slot 2 and Slot 8?
This will help us determine whether the issue is related to a specific slot and whether a previously normal GPU would also experience incorrect temperature detection when installed in the affected slot.

3. IPMI Sensor Query Information
Additionally, if possible, we would appreciate it if you could share the IPMI command or method currently used to query sensor (temperature) information, so that we can further verify the related status.
Thank you very much for your assistance and cooperation. Please feel free to contact us if you have any questions.

Hi Chuan,

1a.) Downgraded IPMI Firmware to 13.05.09: All 4 GPUs are detected, Sensors report 4°C all the time. FANs stay idle.
1b.) Before I downgrade BIOS to F15 Release, can you please confirm that this Release works with our Turin CPU Epyc 9355? I just see Genoa in the Docs.

I will continue testing with 2.) when you confirm that 1b.) is possible with our CPU.

3.) We mostly use Supermicro Systems, so we have SMCIPMITOOL, for other Systems like Gigabyte we use IPMITOOL or Redfish queries. We have a powershell script to start these queries on our technician PCs.
For IPMITool we use e.g.
Ipmitool -I lanplus -H <IP-Address> -U <admin> -P <PASSWORD> sensor
But we also check the values in IPMI Web GUI.
If you need more details I can send you a snippet of our Powershell script.

issues.description:
based on server manual server_manual_G593-SD1-AAX3_e_v1.0.pdf, and the PCIe devices enumeration on the server, the PCIe slotting is not correct for the GPU, the NIC and the Nvme.

1) it is mandatory to have for the NIC: 
0000:18:00.0 : SLOT1     ( currently on SLOT2)
0000:29:00.0 : SLOT2     ( currently on SLOT1)
0000:3a:00.0 : SLOT3     ( currently on SLOT3)
0000:5c:00.0 : SLOT4
0000:9a:00.0 : SLOT5     ( currently on SLOT7)
0000:aa:00.0 : SLOT6     ( currently on SLOT5)
0000:ba:00.0 : SLOT7     ( currently on SLOT8)
0000:da:00.0 : SLOT8     ( currently on SLOT6)

2) to allow GPU direct transfer (affinity on PCIe switch), it is mandatory to have:
SLOT1 and GPU0 (19:00.0) / NVME0 (1a:00.0) connected to the same PCIe switch 
SLOT2 and GPU1 (2a:00.0) / NVME1 (2b:00.0) connected to the same PCIe switch 
SLOT3 and GPU2 (3b:00.0) / NVME2 (3c:00.0) connected to the same PCIe switch 
SLOT4 and GPU3 (5d:00.0) / NVME3 (5e:00.0) connected to the same PCIe switch 
SLOT5 and GPU4 (9b:00.0) / NVME4 (9c:00.0) connected to the same PCIe switch 
SLOT6 and GPU5 (ab:00.0) / NVME5 (ac:00.0) connected to the same PCIe switch 
SLOT7 and GPU6 (bb:00.0) / NVME6 (bc:00.0) connected to the same PCIe switch 
SLOT8 and GPU7 (db:00.0) / NVME7 (dc:00.0) connected to the same PCIe switch 

3) Moroever the CPU NUMA node are not correct.

it is mandatory to have (with 4 NUMA nodes, BIOS parameter SNC set to 2) :
SLOT1 and GPU0 (19:00.0) / NVME0 (1a:00.0) on the same NUMA node 0 
SLOT2 and GPU1 (2a:00.0) / NVME1 (2b:00.0) on the same NUMA node 0 
SLOT3 and GPU2 (3b:00.0) / NVME2 (3c:00.0) on the same NUMA node 1 
SLOT4 and GPU3 (5d:00.0) / NVME3 (5e:00.0) on the same NUMA node 1 
SLOT5 and GPU4 (9b:00.0) / NVME4 (9c:00.0) on the same NUMA node 2 
SLOT6 and GPU5 (ab:00.0) / NVME5 (ac:00.0) on the same NUMA node 2 
SLOT7 and GPU6 (bb:00.0) / NVME6 (bc:00.0) on the same NUMA node 3 
SLOT8 and GPU7 (db:00.0) / NVME7 (dc:00.0) on the same NUMA node 3  
 
4) the Physical Slot field in the PCIe structure should be set accordingly :
PCIe devices SLOT1 / GPU0 / NVME0 -> Physical Slot 0
PCIe devices SLOT2 / GPU1 / NVME1 -> Physical Slot 1
...
PCIe devices SLOT8 / GPU7 / NVME7 -> Physical Slot 7

currently the settings found are :
--> sudo lspci -s 19:00.0 -vv | head -n 3; sudo lspci -s 2a:00.0 -vv | head -n 3; sudo lspci -s 3b:00.0 -vv | head -n 3; sudo lspci -s 5d:00.0 -vv | head -n 3; sudo lspci -s 9b:00.0 -vv | head -n 3; sudo lspci -s ab:00.0 -vv | head -n 3; sudo lspci -s bb:00.0 -vv | head -n 3; sudo lspci -s db:00.0 -vv | head -n 3

19:00.0 3D controller: NVIDIA Corporation GH100 [H200 SXM 141GB] (rev a1)
        Subsystem: NVIDIA Corporation Device 18be
        Physical Slot: 4
2a:00.0 3D controller: NVIDIA Corporation GH100 [H200 SXM 141GB] (rev a1)
        Subsystem: NVIDIA Corporation Device 18be
        Physical Slot: 4-1
3b:00.0 3D controller: NVIDIA Corporation GH100 [H200 SXM 141GB] (rev a1)
        Subsystem: NVIDIA Corporation Device 18be
        Physical Slot: 2
5d:00.0 3D controller: NVIDIA Corporation GH100 [H200 SXM 141GB] (rev a1)
        Subsystem: NVIDIA Corporation Device 18be
        Physical Slot: 2-1
9b:00.0 3D controller: NVIDIA Corporation GH100 [H200 SXM 141GB] (rev a1)
        Subsystem: NVIDIA Corporation Device 18be
        Physical Slot: 2-2
ab:00.0 3D controller: NVIDIA Corporation GH100 [H200 SXM 141GB] (rev a1)
        Subsystem: NVIDIA Corporation Device 18be
        Physical Slot: 2-3

notes.text:
To clarify a bit the NIC issue:

The pci address numbering is not matching the physical numbering in the server manual. Let's take the example of the 2 first NIC:
0000:18:00.0 : SLOT1 ( currently on SLOT2)
0000:29:00.0 : SLOT2 ( currently on SLOT1)

0000:18:00.0 is the first PCI port seen by the OS, so the NIC is named mlnx5_0, but physically on the motherboard it's the NIC in the SLOT2 (indicated in the bloc diagram in the server manual).
Same for 0000:29:00.0, second PCI port seen by the OS, the NIC is named mlnx5_1, so we should expect to be the physical port SLOT2, but in fact it refers to the SLOT1.

See the block diagram attached.

Due to this we cannot identify physically a specific NIC with it's PCI addres or OS interface number and relate it to a physical PCI slot. It's a real problem if need to identify a faulty NIC and replace it.

Regards

Please refer to the attachment
Issue1:
Based on our tests on the actual system, when the NIC is physically inserted into SLOT 1, both the system SMBIOS and the PCIe enumeration consistently point to 0000:4d:00.0. This fully matches the latest reference table provided by RD (see attached image).
Regarding your mention of 0000:18:00.0 corresponding to SLOT 1, this differs from the current hardware wiring design. To ensure we are referencing the same baseline, could you please provide the PCIe Tree diagram from your manual? 

Regarding the other issues, I still see discrepancies between the bus IDs you provided and the bus IDs as I understand them. I am currently discussing this with the EE team.

grep "000" /sys/bus/pci/slots/*/address

Hello Daniel,
As far as I understand your comment, you suggest to set up the affinity between NIC/GPU/NVME by  software.
The concern is that the mlx driver number the NIC in ascending PCI numbering order, which does not correspond to the SLOT numbering order. This makes maintenance very difficult. 
The NIC PCI numbering order must reflect the SLOT numbering. Making the same ordering for GPU and NVME will drastically improves hardware/software maintenance as well.
Regards

Regarding your concern about the discrepancy between NIC PCI numbering and the physical SLOT order, we fully understand the maintenance challenges this may cause.

Based on the attached G593-SD1 topology, the devices are connected via four PCIe G5 Switches (PEX 0-3). Due to the hardware scanning sequence of the PCIe tree, the OS-assigned Bus IDs and mlx interface names do not align linearly with the physical SLOT numbers.

To facilitate your GDR (GPUDirect RDMA) setup, please follow the Mapping Table in  Figure 2 (red box on the right). It provides the exact correlation for each slot:

SLOT 1 maps to mlx5_1 (linked with GPU3/NVME2)
SLOT 2 maps to mlx5_0 (linked with GPU2/NVME0)
(And so forth...)

這個問題其實可以分成兩個層次來確認：

1️⃣ OS 看到的 NUMA 拓撲對不對？
2️⃣ 這個 NUMA 分配是否符合我們硬體設計的 PCIe topology？

你可以按照下面步驟「反向驗證」客戶說的是否正確。

✅ Step 1：在 OS 層確認 GPU 對應的 NUMA node

請客戶在 Linux 下執行：

nvidia-smi topo -m


這個指令會顯示：

GPU 之間的連線

每張 GPU 對應的 CPU NUMA affinity

重點看右邊的 CPU Affinity / NUMA Affinity

或者更精準一點，請客戶跑：

for i in {0..7}; do
  echo "GPU $i"
  cat /sys/bus/pci/devices/$(nvidia-smi -q | grep -A2 "GPU $i" | grep "Bus Id" | awk '{print $5}' | sed 's/00000000://')/numa_node
done


� 這會直接讀：

/sys/bus/pci/devices/xxxx/numa_node


這是 kernel 真正判定的 NUMA node

✅ Step 2：確認 PCIe bus ID 對應的 CPU socket

再請客戶執行：

lspci -tv


或：

lspci -vvv | grep -i numa


你要確認：

GPU 掛在哪個 Root Port

該 Root Port 屬於哪顆 CPU

✅ Step 3：確認 CPU NUMA 本身是否正確

先看系統 NUMA layout：

numactl -H


確認：

node0 有哪些 CPU cores

node1 有哪些

node2 / node3 是不是 NPS=4 模式？

⚠️ 很多 2-socket AMD EPYC 系統會是：

2 socket

每顆 CPU NPS=2 或 NPS=4

所以會出現 4 個 NUMA node

� 關鍵判斷邏輯

你要確認三件事：

1️⃣ GPU 是否均勻分布在 CPU0 / CPU1 底下？

理論上 8 GPU 2 socket：

CPU0 → 4 GPU

CPU1 → 4 GPU

如果變成：

node0 : 3 GPU
node1 : 1 GPU
node2 : 3 GPU
node3 : 1 GPU

這通常代表：

PCIe switch routing 不均

BIOS NUMA mapping 有問題

或 NPS 設定影響

2️⃣ 是否 BIOS 設成 NPS4？

請確認 BIOS：

NUMA Nodes Per Socket (NPS)


如果是 NPS4，
每顆 CPU 會切成 4 個 NUMA node。

這樣 GPU 分布就可能變成：

3-1-3-1

這其實「可能是正常行為」。

3️⃣ GPU 是否跨 socket？

用：

nvidia-smi topo -m


如果 GPU 0–3 都是 PIX or PXB
而 GPU 4–7 是另一組

那其實是正常的，只是 NUMA 切法不同。

issues.summary: RE: SSD Hot-Swap on AMD Mainboards
issues.description:
Hi Daniel,

currently I am testing the GIGABYTE MZ33-AR1 and GIGABYTE MZ73-LM0 in combination with the latest AIC Chassis we want to use.

One of the most important points to test is the ability of hot swapping SSDs.



Unfortunately, with these boards, the hot swap function is not (always) available.

The test results are as followed (all cabled onboard):

-MZ33-AR1:

-NVMe Hot Swap in Linux: works
-NVMe Hot Swap in Windows Server 2025: Doesn’t work

-SATA Hot Swap in Linux: Doesn’t work
-SATA Hot Swap in WS25: Works


-MZ73-LM0:

-NVMe Hot Swap in Linux: Doesn’t work
-NVMe Hot Swap in Windows Server 2025: Doesn’t work

-SATA Hot Swap in Linux: Doesn’t work
-SATA Hot Swap in WS25: Doesn’t work


However, if you attach the disks to an HBA, hot swap always works, so it seems that the cause for the combinations not working is related to the Onboard-Ports.


Is there any BIOS setting we need to change to get hot swap working? Or is this feature generally not supported by default?



Thanks and Best Regards,
Tim

Customer Thomas-krenn (EU) Issue ID 7603

* Problem: Hot swap functionality is inconsistent on GIGABYTE MZ33-AR1 and MZ73-LM0 boards. 
    * MZ33-AR1: Works in Linux (NVMe), doesn't work in Windows Server 2025 (NVMe). SATA hot swap works in WS25, not Linux.
    * MZ73-LM0: Hot swap doesn't work in either Linux or Windows Server 2025 (both NVMe & SATA).
    * Hot swap *always* works when using an HBA, indicating an issue with onboard ports.
    * Initial drive LED issue reported, potentially related to VPP address (Intel VPP address 0x40 suggested & tested successfully on MZ33-AR1 with backplane dip switch adjustment).
    * MS33-AR1 requires a custom BIOS to support hot swap. Customer accepts custom BIOS. BPB pin define needs review.

* Next Action: EE to review BPB pin define. BIOS team to work on custom BIOS for MS33-AR1. Further testing of MZ33-AR1 with 0x40 VPP setting.

* LastActivityFromCreation: 38 days
* Status: assigned

notes.text:
Dear James, 

Thanks for reaching out. 

Please refer to my summary below for this issue:
You believe the BMC version 12.61.15 shipped with R272-P33 is still vulnerable for CVE-2024-54085. And you checked that the BMC 12.61.12 and higher is still vulnerable for CVE-2024-54085 even though it is supposed to be fixed as mentioned in our link below:
https://www.gigabyte.com/Support/Security/2273

If I understand it correctly above, could you please share the detail steps and evidence that indicate the issue is not fixed?

Best regards, 
Brent Lin

Dear Brent,
This is what the customer said:
We could not find the BMC admin password. Either it was changed by someone, or we didn't found the right one (most likely). While searching, I found the blog post on the vuln and a exploit, so given it looked safe enough (that's 1 line of python from a reputable source), I tried it just in case because worst case, I would lose 30 seconds. Turn out it worked.

So that is the evidence.
Best
James

Dear James, 

Thank you for your reply. 

Could you please share the 1-line python code with us to ensure that we are using the same way to reproduce the issue?

Note: For the BMC admin password, please advice the customer to find it from the Chassis label from the side panel. 

Best regards, 
Brent Lin

 Michael!

issues.summary: GPU服务器设备风扇故障咨询
issues.description:
1.升级GPU FAN FW的具体操作步骤和对应工具？
2、整个升级过程需要多长时间?
3、是否不中断业务(在线升级)，还是需要把服务器下线(升级过程中需要重启)才能升级?
4、此类故障是否全球性的已知BUG，现在统计故障率是多少，风扇平均运行多久会出发这个BUG？
5、1-2个风扇故障情况下是否影响整机性能，是否有应急处置方案？

Firmware:
BIOS Version: 1.03
BMC Version: 1.05
CMC Version:

notes.text:
1. 我們沒有提供GPU FAN FW的更新
2. 同1.
3. 同1.
4. 風扇良率太差，可以跟BD Leo回報，我方可以就良率問題跟原廠討論
請提供相關數量及數據
風扇P/N、該風扇故障數量 (GPU機器有多種風扇，請務必區分好P/N及數量)
期間如有需要RMA，可以申請RMA
如有需要買備品，請聯絡Thomas商談
5. G894機器的風扇沒有redundancy的設計，1~2個風扇故障可能導致GPU過熱
且該風扇不支援hot-swap，機器運行中拔除可能導致整機throttle，請務必關機斷電後再進行風扇的更換

custom_fields.value:
Customer Dimension AI (EU) Issue ID 7873

* Problem: GPU fan firmware updates are NOT provided. Fan failure rate is poor; report quantities & P/N to BD Leo for factory discussion. 1-2 fan failures on G894 servers can cause GPU overheating. Fans are NOT hot-swappable – server MUST be powered off before replacement to avoid throttling. No interruption-free (online) upgrade path; server restart required.
* Next Action: Collect GPU fan P/N and failure quantities. Report to BD Leo. Initiate RMA process if needed. Contact Thomas for spare parts.
* LastActivityFromCreation: 0 days
* Status: assigned

issues.summary: The issue where the GPU clock is locked at 1GHz and the issue where thermal throttling occurs.
issues.description:
1. This machine has been experiencing an issue where the GPU clock is locked at 1GHz for over two years.
After booting this machine, using the GPU causes random GPUs to randomly encounter this problem at random times.

2. This machine has been experiencing high temperatures, causing thermal throttling and resulting in reduced GPU performance. Checking SEL showed all fans running at 21600 RPM.

This machine is equipped with four H100 units.
It is unclear if these two issues are related, but I would like to know a solution.


Firmware:
BIOS Version: 
BMC Version: 
CMC Version:

notes.text:
I was able to find out the BIOS and BMC versions, so I'll let you know.

BIOS：R07
BMC：12.61.17

I have confirmed from the logs you submitted that this server has four H100 servers installed.
Since the H100 is not included in the QVL for this server model, please replace the GPU and confirm that the problem is related to the server before reporting the issue.

The SEL log provided in the email has been uploaded.

Please also confirm the actual installation location of the GPU card, and whether the problem persists after changing the installation location.

We are currently awaiting a response from the customer.
We are requesting that customers take a photo of the slot where the GPU is physically installed.
However, we are concerned about how to proceed if a customer refuses this request.

Can we check the PCIe slot location from this PCIe bus info?

GPU 00000000:01:00.0

Nakazawa-san,

Please try to get the actual H100 installed slot location info.

from nvidia_smi_q.txt...

I can see the two GPU has high temp and drop the Average Power Draw.
        GPU Current Temp                  : 85 C

This is PCIe Inventory.

Can we check the H100 installed location from our shipping record.

Who will carry this out?

Customer Kerno (Middle East) Issue ID 7754

* Problem: Server R283-Z96-AAE2 experiences fan noise due to the BMC not detecting fan speed on boot. A BMC cold reset temporarily resolves the issue, but it recurs after each reboot or driver installation. Firmware updates (BIOS & BMC) have provided limited improvement. Logs show no errors, and fan cage replacement didn’t fix the problem. The issue persists regardless of power supply population or CMOS clearing. Initial investigation suggests a potential issue with the fan speed IC not returning correct values to the BMC.
* Next Action: Gain VPN access to the customer’s environment to directly verify fan speed IC readings to the BMC. Explore replicating the issue in a lab environment. Await thermal team’s updated fan profile for SKU build and update.
* LastActivityFromCreation: 18 days
* Status: assigned

issues.summary: FAN profile of the R263-Z37 does not operate correctly.
issues.description:
We are experiencing an issue where the FAN profile of the R263-Z37 does not operate correctly.
As shown in the attached screenshots, although the memory temperature has reached 75°C, the fan speed remains at approximately 5,000 RPM, and as a result, the memory is not being adequately cooled.
We have confirmed that this issue does not occur on another unit with the same barebone within our company, where the fan speed increased to up to 10,000 RPM under similar conditions.
Since we performed a BMC firmware update before observing this behavior, we are unsure whether the issue existed prior to the update.
Could this be a motherboard-related failure?
If there are any other possible corrective actions or troubleshooting steps, we would appreciate your guidance.


Firmware:
BIOS Version: 
BMC Version: 
CMC Version:

notes.text: Please include the server's serial number, current configuration, and firmware version.

The configuration is as follows.
Motherboard:R263-Z37-AAH1
S/N:PG8NJ900147
BIOS: R30_F44
BMC: 13.06.18
Configuration :EPYC9115 x1, 64GB DDR5-4800 memory x8, 1.92TB SSD x2

Could you please specify which BMC firmware versions are involved in the update (from which version to which)?

If a unit of the same model exhibits normal fan speeds, please download the fan Profile  file from that unit. Apply and activate this Profile  on the affected device, then perform a reboot to verify if the issue improves.

The BMC firmware was updated from version 13.06.16 to 13.06.18.

After downloading the barebone fan profile and importing it into the motherboard, the fan speeds of FAN1A and FAN2A increased, and the memory temperature decreased.
However, even after importing the original motherboard profile that had been backed up earlier, the fan speed did not return to the original level (5,000 RPM), and the issue no longer reproduced.

As a result, the problem has been resolved, but we would appreciate it if you could investigate the root cause of this behavior.

Understood. Confirmed that fan speeds returned to normal after importing the functional fan profile; thank you for the verification.

Regarding the investigation into the root cause: please provide both the functional fan profile (with normal speeds) and the problematic profile (where fan speeds failed to revert). Will consult with the engineering team to analyze this behavior once received. Thank you.

We are sending the fan profile. Please confirm.
fanprofile.json: Original R263-Z37 fanprofile (FAN speed abnormal) 
fanprofile2.json:Barebonejson fanprofile
fanprofile3.json:Current R263-Z37 fanprofile (FAN speed normal)

Thank you for providing the fan profile configuration files. Upon review of the six files, the two files within "fanprofile3" show different definitions compared to the others. The operation for the CPU temperature sensor is set to full speed (100%) once temperatures exceed 30°C.

Additionally, following a BMC firmware update, settings are overwritten and not retained, leaving only the default configuration for that version. The fan profile must be reloaded and activated to take effect. Due to the requirement for manual loading, there is a possibility that profiles with similar names but different settings were selected, which may account for the perceived decrease in fan speed.

Thank you for your response.
However, we were unable to fully understand the explanation provided.

Could you please clarify whether this issue is a BMC firmware bug, or whether it was caused by an issue with the customer’s BMC update procedure?
We would appreciate a more detailed explanation.

Checking a few more details for clarification:
1.Was the BMC firmware updated via the Web UI?
2.If updated via Web UI, was the "Preserve Configuration" option selected (it is unchecked by default)?
3.After the BMC update, is the active profile in Fan Profile your custom configuration?

1.Was the BMC firmware updated via the Web UI?
2.If updated via Web UI, was the "Preserve Configuration" option selected (it is unchecked by default)?
Since the update was performed via the UEFI shell, the “Preserve Configuration” option is unknown.

3.After the BMC update, is the active profile in Fan Profile your custom configuration?
It is set to the default fan profile.

4. Others
The customer performed the firmware update with Broadcom 9580-8i8e, fs MCX515A-CCAT, and GIGABYTE CLNCB2 installed.
Is there any possible relation to this issue?

>>It is set to the default fan profile.
During the BMC update via UEFI, there is an option to retain existing settings.

If "Y" is selected: Custom fan profiles are preserved, and the active custom profile remains unchanged.

If "N" is selected: Custom fan profiles are cleared, and the system reverts to the default fan profile.

Since the system defaulted to the standard profile after the update, it indicates that the settings were not retained. In this scenario, custom fan profiles must be reloaded manually. The observation regarding a potential "speed decrease" is likely due to the non-retained settings or the selection of a similar profile during manual recovery, rather than an issue caused by the update itself.

>>Others:
 BMC version 13.06.24 has been released. To address any concerns regarding BMC stability or performance, it is highly recommended to update to this latest version to ensure optimal operation.

Regarding the comment below:
“The observation regarding a potential ‘speed decrease’ is likely due to the non-retained settings.”
What the customer is currently concerned about is that the memory temperature is high, but the fan speed remains fixed at 5000 RPM and does not increase.
When updating the BMC firmware, if “N” is selected, wouldn’t the fan profile be cleared and the fans switch back to automatic control?
On the other hand, if “Y” is selected, the fan profile would be preserved, and the fan speed values could remain fixed at a specific RPM (xxxx RPM).
Would it be correct to explain this behavior to the customer in this way?
Also, shouldn’t this be identifiable by checking the fan profile?

>>When updating the BMC firmware, if “N” is selected, wouldn’t the fan profile be cleared and the fans switch back to automatic control?


 The system reverts to the default fan profile.

>>On the other hand, if “Y” is selected, the fan profile would be preserved, and the fan speed values could remain fixed at a specific RPM (xxxx RPM).
Would it be correct to explain this behavior to the customer in this way?

The system use fan profile is last state (remains unchanged.)


>>Also, shouldn’t this be identifiable by checking the fan profile?

The retention status and activation of the fan profile can be confirmed through the BMC settings.

Since my original question does not seem to have been fully understood, I would like to clarify it again.
The issue the customer is concerned about is that the memory temperature becomes high, but the fan speed remains fixed at 5,000 RPM and does not increase.

Please allow me to ask the following questions:
1.The customer’s question is: why does the fan speed not increase even though the memory temperature is high?
2.We have provided the fan profile—is it possible to identify the root cause of this issue based on that information?
3.By comparing the initial fan profile values, the barebone default values, and the latest captured values, can any potential cause be identified?
4.From the fan profile, is it possible to determine whether “Y” or “N” was selected during the BMC update?
Since GIGA Computing should know the default fan profile values, we assume that any differences should be recognizable.
5.Or is this behavior possibly caused by a BMC firmware bug?

The customer performed additional verification.

The issue was reproduced under the following conditions:
when three or more cards are installed in the PCIe slots, at least two of which are 9580-8i8e cards, and a card is installed in the bottom-left slot when viewed from the rear. In this configuration, changing the FAN profile via the WebUI does not take effect.

Under these conditions, even after applying system load for approximately 30 minutes, neither the memory temperature nor the FAN speed changes when monitored via the WebUI or by issuing ipmitool commands.

However, after about 10–15 minutes, a change in fan noise can be observed audibly, although the FAN speed reported by the WebUI and ipmitool does not change.

Therefore, it is questionable whether the sensors are correctly reading and reporting the temperature and fan speed values.

>>4.From the fan profile, is it possible to determine whether “Y” or “N” was selected during the BMC update?
Regrettably, the BMC function does not include a feature to record such settings. 

>>5.Or is this behavior possibly caused by a BMC firmware bug?
Thank you for the verification. A similar issue has recently been identified, though it differs slightly from the current description. Since the issue was also observed in version 13.06.18,Please update to version 13.06.24 to ensure any known bugs are addressed. Access the download here:

https://www.gigabyte.com/jp/Enterprise/Rack-Server/R263-Z37-AAH2-rev-3x#Support-Firmware

If the problem persists after the update, a reproduction environment will be set up to confirm the issue and escalate it to the relevant department for further analysis.

>Please update to version 13.06.24 to ensure any known bugs are addressed.
We have received a report from the customer that they updated to version 13.06.24, but the issue was not resolved.
We would also like you to continue investigating the issue where the fan profile cannot be changed when cards are installed in the PCIe slots.

Please also provide your responses to the following items.
1.The customer’s question is: why does the fan speed not increase even though the memory temperature is high?
2.We have provided the fan profile—is it possible to identify the root cause of this issue based on that information?
3.By comparing the initial fan profile values, the barebone default values, and the latest captured values, can any potential cause be identified?

>>1.The customer’s question is: why does the fan speed not increase even though the memory temperature is high?
Based on the description provided, the fan speed reduction occurred following the firmware update. Since the current BMC FW version is not the latest, this issue may be resolved by updating if it is a previously addressed bug.

>>2.We have provided the fan profile—is it possible to identify the root cause of this issue based on that information?
>>3.By comparing the initial fan profile values, the barebone default values, and the latest captured values, can any potential cause be identified?
Organized the fanprofile configuration files provided. While there are minor differences in sensor and fan naming, only Policy-0 in two of the profiles is set to 100% fan speed; other settings remain largely consistent.

Please remember to "Preserve Configuration" during the BMC update to avoid clearing custom files. Default speeds will differ from the custom settings.

Current issue
The fan speed does not change immediately even after modifying the FAN profile.
This issue was not resolved after updating the BMC version to 13.06.24.
Even after waiting for several tens of minutes, the changes are not reflected in either the ipmitool command output or the WebUI.
However, after rebooting the OS, changes in the fan speed values can be observed in both ipmitool and the WebUI.
This issue occurs when PCIe cards are installed in the PCIe slots.
When no PCIe cards are installed, the issue does not occur.

Values after OS reboot
```
CPU0_TEMP                | 49.000     | degrees C  | ok    | na        | na        | na        | 97.000    | 100.000   | na        
BPB_FAN_1A               | 9150.000   | RPM        | ok    | na        | 750.000   | 1050.000  | na        | na        | na        
BPB_FAN_2A               | 9150.000   | RPM        | ok    | na        | 750.000   | 1050.000  | na        | na        | na        
BPB_FAN_3A               | 9150.000   | RPM        | ok    | na        | 750.000   | 1050.000  | na        | na        | na        
BPB_FAN_4A               | 5400.000   | RPM        | ok    | na        | 750.000   | 1050.000  | na        | na        | na        
CPU0_Status              | 0x0        | discrete   | 0x8080| na        | na        | na        | na        | na        | na        
DIMMG0_TEMP              | 64.000     | degrees C  | ok    | na        | na        | na        | 85.000    | 87.000    | na        
DIMMG1_TEMP              | 59.000     | degrees C  | ok    | na        | na        | na        | 85.000    | 87.000    | na       
We are attaching the FAN profile file used at that time: fanprofile_4.json.

Thank you for the report and the provided files. Will attempt to replicate the issue using a machine of the same model.

Regarding the previous report, does the issue only trigger when three or more PCIe cards are connected, or can it be reproduced with just one card?

If possible, please provide screenshots of the PCI Inventory and NIC Inventory from the Web UI for reference. Thank you.

Attempted to reproduce the issue using an identical model; however, the symptoms could not be replicated. Compared the provided fanprofile_4.json and fanprofile_3-2.json for testing.

The configurations retrieved from these files have been organized into the spreadsheet tab: Fan Profile_Customer.

Upon importing fanprofile_4.json, observed that "default_a1876" is set to "RUN" status by default. Please note that when downloading a configuration file, it captures the profile currently in "RUN" status. For comparison, analyzed the five previously provided files, which all default to "RUN" for the "default" profile upon loading. These details are in the tab: Fan Profile_import.

Test results are documented in the Contrast tab, covering three scenarios: no PCIe card, one PCIe card, and three PCIe cards, using both provided JSON files. The report lists PCIe slot positions, card status, fan profile settings, and temperature/airflow logs.

Fan profile behavior aligns with the defined settings; no abnormalities were found.

Report is attached for your review.

If the issue persists, please provide more detailed information, such as current slot occupancy and screenshots of the PCIe/NIC inventory. 

Does the issue persist when using different NIC models?

If possible, please provide detailed, step-by-step instructions to reproduce the issue. This will assist in replicating the situation on this end. Thank you.

Thank you for performing the reproduction test.
We have requested the customer to proceed with the items you asked for, so could you please wait for a little while?

The correct files are as follows:
fanprofile.json
fanprofile_2.json
fanprofile_3.json
The following files are not required, as the same files were uploaded multiple times:
fanprofile-2.json
fanprofile_2-2.json
fanprofile_3-2.json

Customer Verification Results:
When a NIC is installed in slot 6 and two 9580-8i8e cards are installed in slots 1 and 5, the fan speed issue occurs (fan speed does not immediately reflect profile changes).
When all installed cards are NICs, the fan speed issue does not occur under these conditions.
If the configuration does not include two or more 9580-8i8e cards, the issue does not occur even when a card is installed in Slot 6.
Even when all four rear slots are populated, the issue does not occur unless two or more 9580-8i8e cards are installed.

Step-by-Step Verification
1.Install OCP and NIC in Slot 7 and Slot 5
→ No issue. After changing the profile, the fan speed is immediately reflected.
2.Add one 9580-8i8e to Slot 1 (in addition to the above configuration)
→ No issue. After changing the profile, the fan speed is immediately reflected.
3.Add one 9580-8i8e to Slot 6 (in addition to the above configuration)
→ Issue occurs. After changing the profile, the fan speed is not reflected.
4.Move the 9580-8i8e from Slot 6 to Slot 3
→ No issue. After changing the profile, the fan speed is immediately reflected.
5.Install NIC in Slot 6 and 9580-8i8e in Slot 1 and Slot 5
→ Issue occurs. After changing the profile, the fan speed is not reflected.
6.Return to the initial failure configuration (NIC in Slot 5, 9580-8i8e in Slot 1, Slot 3, and Slot 6)
→ Issue occurs. After changing the profile, the fan speed is not reflected.

Please find the attached files for your reference.

Thank you for providing the cross-testing results. These results have been discussed with the Product Engineer, and the response is as follows:

The Barebone design specifications for this product support the configuration of only one RAID card. Please refer to the attached diagram for details.

Based on the above explanation, it aligns with the information provided: anomalies occur when two RAID cards are installed simultaneously. Therefore, it is not recommended to use two RAID cards in this system. 

Please advise the customer that this Barebone model does not support a second RAID card.

Thank you for your response.

Instead of an image, do you have any information available in PDF format or on a website?
We would like to provide PDF documents or official website information to the customer.

Since the screenshot previously provided contained internal document information as part of the authorization, I cannot provide the entire document for reference.

I have submitted a request for supplemental updates regarding the website descriptions for this case; however, please understand that updating the information on the website will take time.

Additionally, as Barebone shipments are not customized products, we will not restrict how end customers use them. Nonetheless, please understand that Barebones are subject to certain functional limitations due to their mechanical design and wiring.

Regarding the functional aspects, please also explain to the end customer that due to configuration constraints, the barebone system cannot support all LAN and RAID cards simultaneously. Priority has been given to LAN card compatibility; therefore, the system can only support one RAID card due to these design trade-offs.

While there are no restrictions on how end customers use the product, please contact our sales team to initiate a customization request if there are specific requirements. Thank you.

We have received the following questions from the customer:

1.Does this customization mean creating a customized version of the BIOS or BMC? Or does it mean customization at the hardware level?
2.Will there be any additional cost involved?

3.Regarding the statement that the website will be updated with additional information for this case (supporting only one RAID card), does this also apply to other Barebone models?
Since this is one of the criteria for purchasing decisions, we would also like to request that the information be reviewed and updated for other applicable Barebone models, if any.

1.This project specifically refers to the customized BMC portion.

2.Please be advised that the customization of the BMC will incur additional charges.

3.As the design of each barebone system varies slightly, we recommend specifying any special requirements at the time of purchase. Our sales team and BDM will then recommend models that meet your configuration needs and provide notification of any additional costs.
Based on the current review results, the website and documentation may not be updated. This is because usage and configuration requirements are typically addressed during the procurement process, at which point we will clarify specifications and advise on any potential extra fees.

custom_fields.value:
Customer Sunway (JP) Issue ID 7694

* Problem: R263-Z37 fan speed remains at 5000 RPM despite high memory temperature (75°C). Issue began after BMC firmware update (13.06.16 to 13.06.18). Importing a functional fan profile temporarily resolved the issue, but the original speed wasn't restored. Further testing revealed the issue occurs when two or more 9580-8i8e RAID cards are installed, specifically with a NIC in slot 6. The root cause is a barebone design limitation: the system is only designed to support *one* RAID card due to hardware constraints. Changing the fan profile via WebUI doesn't take effect immediately; a reboot is required.
* Next Action: Inform customer the barebone doesn’t support two RAID cards. Advise contacting sales for customization if needed (BMC modification - additional cost).
* LastActivityFromCreation: 26 days
* Status: assigned
